# **I. Overview** {#i.-overview}

* Project code: VCP  
* Group name: Nhóm 1  
* Software type: Mobile App

# **II. Requirement Specification** {#ii.-requirement-specification}

## ***II.1 Problem description*** {#ii.1-problem-description}

The VNeID Civic Point System is a strategic module integrated into the National Population Database application (VNeID). While the digital infrastructure for public services exists, the current adoption rate and the habit of maintaining up-to-date digital data among citizens need improvement.

The problem this system addresses is the lack of a standardized, gamified mechanism to measure and incentivize citizens' digital engagement. Currently, there is no system to track how active a citizen is in the digital space or to reward them for contributing to digital governance.

The VNeID Civic Point System aims to encourage citizens to actively participate in the national digital transformation program. The system operates on the principle of encouragement, not punishment. It tracks specific digital behaviors—such as perfecting personal profiles, performing online administrative procedures, and participating in social governance—and converts them into "Civic Points." Based on these points, citizens are ranked and granted tangible financial benefits, such as tax reductions and fee waivers.

## ***II.2 Major Features***  {#ii.2-major-features}

FE-01: Manage Digital Identity and Profile Verification Track and verify citizen profile completeness on VNeID, including document integration and digital signature usage, to award identity-related points.  
FE-02: Track Digital Service Activities Record citizen activities across online administrative procedures, financial transactions, e-commerce, social networking, and other VNeID-authenticated digital services for point calculation.  
FE-03: Track Healthcare and Education Digital Services Record healthcare service usage (appointment booking, result retrieval) and digital literacy course completion certified through integrated healthcare and education platforms.  
FE-04: Manage Civic Engagement and Social Contributions Track citizen participation in legal draft contributions, parliamentary interactions, government surveys, and complaint submissions to state agencies.  
FE-05: Calculate Civic Points with Anti-Fraud Logic Automatically calculate points from verified data sources, enforce single-count priority rule to prevent duplicates, and manage annual 30% point retention and conditional 100-point profile bonus.  
FE-06: Classify Citizen Ranks Dynamically Classify citizens into Active Digital Citizen (≥ 350), Basic Digital Citizen (100–349), or Unranked (\< 100\) based on accumulated points to determine incentive eligibility.  
FE-07: Apply Tax and Fee Incentives Export eligibility data for tax and fee reductions (Personal Income Tax, Registration Fee, Value Added Tax) to Tax Authority and receive applied reduction confirmations.  
FE-08: Support Vulnerable Groups Identify elderly, disabled, and ethnic minority citizens via VNeID Core and automatically allocate 50 bonus points per year upon reaching the 150-point minimum threshold.  
FE-09: Handle Violations and Point Suspension Monitor violation records from authority systems, trigger warnings, and temporarily suspend point accumulation until resolution is confirmed.  
FE-10: Manage Citizen Notifications and Alerts Deliver timely notifications to citizens for point updates, rank changes, warnings, suspensions, suspension lifts, and incentive eligibility status.  
FE-11: Manage Citizen Point Inquiry and Feedback Allow citizens to view point balance, point history, rank status, and submit feedback or complaints regarding their point records.  
FE-12: Configure Scoring Rules and Parameters Enable administrators to dynamically add, modify, or disable scoring rules, rank thresholds, retention percentages, and suspension policies without system redeployment.

## II.4 Nonfunctional requirements  {#ii.4-nonfunctional-requirements}

| \# | Feature | System Function | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| NF-01 | API Performance | Response Time Constraint | All user-facing API endpoints (point inquiry, rank retrieval, notification fetch) must return responses within 2s (normal load) and 5s (peak load). |
| NF-02 | System Scalability | Concurrent User Capacity | The system shall support a minimum of 1,000,000 concurrent users accessing point services simultaneously without performance degradation. |
| NF-03 | Data Throughput | Processing Throughput | The Point Scoring and Data Ingestion services must process a minimum of 10,000 activity records per second during peak hours. |
| NF-04 | System Availability | Uptime Constraint | The system shall maintain a minimum availability of 99.9% (unplanned downtime \< 8.76 hours/year), excluding 48h-pre-announced maintenance. |
| NF-05 | Data Encryption | Security & Privacy Standard | All citizen personal data, point balances, and transaction logs must be encrypted using AES-256 at rest and TLS 1.3 during transmission. |
| NF-06 | Disaster Recovery | Geographic Redundancy | Automated failover between 2+ geographically distributed data centers must complete within 15 minutes with zero data loss (RPO \= 0). |
| NF-07 | Audit Persistence | Audit Trail Compliance | Every system event (point calculation, rule change, rank update) must be recorded in an append-only database and retained for at least 5 years. |
| NF-08 | Accessibility | Accessibility Compliance | All interfaces and notifications must comply with WCAG 2.1 Level AA and support full Vietnamese Unicode (UTF-8) for vulnerable groups. |
| NF-09 | Rule Propagation | Cache Refresh Constraint | Modifications to scoring rules or rank thresholds must be refreshed across all system nodes within 30 seconds without requiring a restart. |
| NF-10 | Backup Resilience | Data Backup Reliability | Automated full backups must occur every 6 hours with 90-day retention in geographically redundant storage. |
| NF-11 | Integration Health | Connectivity Monitoring | The system must monitor connectivity of all external platforms every 10 minutes and alert administrators if data is not received within expected intervals. |
| NF-12 | Notification Latency | Dispatch Time Constraint | Notifications for point updates or rank changes must be delivered to the VNeID application within 5 minutes of the triggering event. |

## 

## II.5 Functional requirements {#ii.5-functional-requirements}

### II.5*.1 Actors* {#ii.5.1-actors}

### 

| \# | Actor | Type | Description |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 1 | Citizen | Human (Primary) | A registered VNeID user who views their Digital Citizen score and rank, receives notifications, submits feedback, and checks applicable tax/fee reduction benefits. |
| 2 | System Administrator | Human (Primary) | Manages the system by configuring scoring rules, setting incentive tiers, resolving citizen disputes, and monitoring system-wide analytics. |
| 3 | VNeID Core Identity System | External System | The national identity platform providing authentication, profile data, app engagement logs, vulnerable group flags, and account status updates. |
| 4 | Public Service Platform | External System | Sends records when citizens successfully complete online administrative procedures (Level 4 DVC) via government portals. |
| 5 | Financial Partner | External System | Banks and e-wallets that provide logs of VNeID-authenticated cashless payments for taxes, utilities, and public services. |
| 6 | Healthcare Platform | External System | Supplies verified data regarding digital medical bookings, cashless hospital fee payments, and voluntary blood donations. |
| 7 | Education Platform | External System | Sends certification records when citizens successfully complete state-recognized digital literacy or online safety courses. |
| 8 | Legislative Platform | External System | Records civic participation data, such as citizen feedback on draft legal documents or official government surveys. |
| 9 | Tax & Treasury Authority | External System | Receives the list of citizens eligible for tax/fee reductions and returns confirmation logs once the benefits are successfully applied. |

### 

### II.5.2 Use Cases {#ii.5.2-use-cases}

### II.5.2 .1 Diagram(s) {#ii.5.2-.1-diagram(s)}

![][image4]

![][image5]

![][image6]

### II.5.2.2 Use case descriptions {#ii.5.2.2-use-case-descriptions}

| ID | Use Case | Actors | Use Case Description |
| :---: | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 01 | Authenticate User | Citizen, Admin | Authenticates Citizens via VNeID Level 2 SSO and Admins via the Admin Portal with 2FA. |
| 02 | Inquire Point Balance and Rank | Citizen | Displays current score and Rank: Active (≥350), Basic (100-349), or Unranked (\<100). |
| 03 | Receive System Notifications | Citizen | Delivers real-time push notifications for point additions, rank changes, or incentive eligibility. |
| 04 | Submit Feedback and Complaint | Citizen | Allows citizens to dispute point calculations or report administrative issues through VNeID. |
| 05 | Configure Scoring Rules | Admin | Sets point values (e.g., \+100 for profile, \+7 for public services, \+10 for legal drafts) and the annual 30% retention rule. |
| 06 | Configure Tiered Incentives | Admin | Defines tax/fee reduction rates (e.g., 10%) and the point thresholds for each rank tier. |
| 07 | Resolve Complaints and View Reports | Admin | Manages disputes from UC-04 and views system-wide analytics on citizen rank distribution. |
| 08 | Record Profile Completion | VNeID Core | Records 100% completion of the VNeID base profile (+100 points). |
| 09 | Record Document Integration | VNeID Core | Records the successful digital integration of official cards (Health Insurance, Driver's License, etc.). |
| 10 | Record App Engagement | VNeID Core | Records periodic usage frequency logs to determine digital engagement levels. |
| 11 | Record Public Service Completion | DVC Portal | Records completion of online administrative procedures (Level 4 Public Services). |
| 12 | Record Financial Transactions | Financial Partner | Records cashless payments for taxes or public utilities authenticated through VNeID. |
| 13 | Record Healthcare and Donation | Health Platform | Records medical bookings and voluntary blood donations (linked via Electronic Health Book). |
| 14 | Record Digital Literacy Completion | Edu Platform | Records completion of digital skills and online safety courses. |
| 15 | Record Civic Engagement | Legislative Platform | Records contributions to legal drafts, surveys, or interactions with National Assembly deputies. |
| 16 | Calculate and Add Points | Internal System | Triggers after UC-08 to UC-15 to calculate and credit points to the ledger based on UC-05 rules. |
| 17 | Evaluate Citizen Rank | Internal System | Re-evaluates and assigns the rank (Unranked, Basic, or Active) whenever the point balance changes. |
| 18 | Execute Year-End Processing | Internal System | Performs the Dec 31st reset: calculates 30% carryover points and resets the annual counter. |
| 19 | Allocate Vulnerable Group Bonus | Internal System | Awards \+50 annual bonus points to elderly, disabled, or ethnic minority citizens reaching a 150-point baseline. |
| 20 | Suspend/Resume Accumulation | Internal System | Pauses point credits if the VNeID account is locked/revoked; resumes once the account is restored. |
| 21 | Determine Tax/Fee Reduction Tier | Citizen | Displays the specific 10% reduction eligibility for PIT, Registration Fees, or VAT based on the Active rank. |
| 22 | Export/Import Incentive Data | Tax Authority | Exports the eligible list to the Tax Dept and imports confirmation once the reduction is applied to the citizen. |

UC-01: Authenticate User

| UC ID and Name: | UC-01 – Authenticate User |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen The Son | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | Citizen, System Admin | Secondary Actors: | VNeID Core |
| Trigger: | The user (Citizen or System Administrator) opens the Digital Citizen Point System and requests access to features requiring authorization. |  |  |
| Description: | This use case describes the user authentication process. For Citizens, the system redirects to VNeID Gateway where the citizen authenticates via Level 2 SSO and grants consent for the VCP system to access their identity data (compliant with Decree 13/2023/NĐ-CP). For System Administrators, authentication is performed through the Admin Portal with username/password and Two-Factor Authentication (2FA). After identity verification, the system uses the Citizen Identity Number (Số CCCD) as the sole matching key to identify the citizen, checks internal account status to determine access level (full access or restricted due to suspension per UC-20), and initiates a role-based session. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1	The citizen is an individual with a valid electronic identification account (or a foreigner/person of Vietnamese origin living in Vietnam with an identity certificate and an electronic identification account). PRE-2	The System Administrator has a valid administration account and a device to receive 2FA codes. PRE-3	VNeID Core Identity System is available for SSO verification. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1	The user is successfully authenticated and the system records the user's digital identity. POST-2	The system grants access and displays the functional interface corresponding to the user's role and account status. POST-3	The login event is recorded in the audit log. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | 1	The user initiates the login process based on their role. (a) Citizen selects "Login with VNeID" on the mobile app or web portal → proceed to Step 2\. (b) System Administrator navigates to the Admin Portal → proceed to Step 7\. 2	The system redirects the authentication request to the VNeID Gateway. 3	The Citizen authenticates on VNeID (password, biometric, or other VNeID-supported method) and grants consent for the VCP system to access their identity fields (name, Citizen Identity Number, date of birth), compliant with Decree 13/2023/NĐ-CP. 4	VNeID Core processes the authentication and returns a secure token along with the citizen's authorized identity profile via callback. 5	The system extracts the citizen's unique national identifier (Citizen Identity Number — Số CCCD) from the VNeID token payload and uses it as the sole matching key against the local CITIZEN table. If no match is found, the system auto-provisions a new citizen profile (see AF-3). If a match is found, the system checks the account status (active or suspended per UC-20). 6	The system records the login event in the audit log, initiates a session, and displays the Citizen dashboard. If the account is suspended, the system grants read-only access with a suspension notice (see AF-2). Flow ends. 7	The System Administrator enters their username and password on the Admin Portal. 8	The system verifies the credentials and prompts for a 2FA code. 9	The System Administrator enters a valid 2FA code. The system verifies it. 10	The system records the login event in the audit log, initiates a session with a configured shorter timeout, and displays the Admin dashboard. Flow ends. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1	Citizen denies consent at Step 3\. 	Authentication is aborted. The system displays a message explaining that consent is required to use the VCP system. The citizen is returned to the login screen. No data is retrieved from VNeID. AF-2	Citizen's account is suspended (detected at Step 5).	            The system still allows login but with read-only access only. The dashboard displays a suspension notice: "Your account is currently suspended. You can view your point balance and history, but no new points will be accumulated." The citizen can access UC-02 (view points) and UC-04 (submit complaint) but cannot trigger any point-earning activities. AF-3	Citizen is logging in for the first time (no match found at Step 5).	The system creates a new citizen profile using the CCCD as the primary key, with 0 points, Unranked status, and records the consent grant. The citizen is directed to the dashboard with a welcome message. AF-4	Invalid credentials provided by Citizen.	            VNeID Core returns an authentication error. The system denies access and notifies the citizen to check their identification account. AF-5	Invalid credentials or 2FA code provided by Administrator. 	The system denies access and displays an error. After the configured maximum failed attempts (e.g., 5), the Admin account is temporarily locked for the configured duration (e.g., 15 minutes). Every failed attempt is logged in the audit log. AF-6	Administrator's 2FA device is unavailable. 	The Administrator contacts a Super Admin for a temporary bypass code or alternative 2FA method, following the organization's security recovery procedure. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1	VNeID Core is unresponsive or times out. 	The system displays a connection error and asks the citizen to try again later. Citizen authentication is temporarily unavailable. Admin authentication is not affected. EX-2	Network timeout during VNeID callback (Step 4). 	The system retries once. If it fails again, the citizen is asked to try later. No session is created. EX-3	System error during auto-provisioning (Step 5). 	Authentication succeeds but profile creation fails. The system logs the error, alerts the Administrator, and asks the citizen to try again later. |  |  |
| Priority: | High, Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Very High — Triggered every time a user starts a new access session to the system. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01	Each citizen has only one unique digital identity linked to their national electronic identification account, protected by law. BR-02	Citizens are responsible for securing their electronic identification account information and authentication methods. BR-03	Electronic identification and authentication using VNeID have legal validity for executing digital transactions. BR-04	Citizen consent to share identity data with the VCP system is required at first login and is compliant with Decree 13/2023/NĐ-CP. Consent status is stored and can be revoked by the citizen at any time. If consent is revoked, the system must immediately cease using the citizen's identity data for any external sharing (e.g., UC-22 export). Full handling of consent revocation (profile anonymization, data retention policy) is deferred to a future UC-23 – Manage Privacy Settings. BR-05	Admin session timeout must be configured shorter than Citizen session timeout to ensure system security (e.g., Admin \= 15 minutes idle, Citizen \= 30 minutes idle). BR-06	All failed login attempts (Citizen and Admin) are recorded in the AUDIT\_LOG with timestamp, IP address, and failure reason. BR-07	Suspended citizens can authenticate and access read-only features (view points, submit complaints) but cannot perform any point-earning activities. BR-08	Admin accounts are temporarily locked after exceeding the configured maximum failed login attempts. The lock duration is configurable. BR-09	The citizen's Citizen Identity Number (Số CCCD) returned by VNeID Core is the sole unique identifier used to match and deduplicate citizen records in the VCP system. Other attributes (phone number, email, display name) may change over time and must never be used as matching keys. This prevents duplicate profiles when a citizen updates their contact information. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream	None (entry point of the system) Downstream	All other UCs requiring authenticated access. UC-20 (provides suspension status checked at Step 5). |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1	The VNeID Core system operates continuously and can handle a large volume of authentication transactions. AS-2	All System Administrators have been provisioned with 2FA devices before the system goes live. AS-3	The VNeID Gateway supports the OAuth2/OpenID Connect consent flow required for Decree 13/2023/NĐ-CP compliance. AS-4	Citizens have been informed about the data-sharing consent requirement through the VNeID app's terms of service. AS-5	VNeID Core always returns the citizen's Citizen Identity Number (Số CCCD) as part of the token payload, and this number remains stable throughout the citizen's lifetime. |  |  |

UC-02: Inquire Point Balance and Rank

| UC ID and Name: | UC-02: Inquire Point Balance and Rank |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen The Son | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | Citizen  | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | The Citizen selects the function to view their points and rank on the Digital Citizen Point System interface |  |  |
| Description: | This use case allows the Citizen to look up their current accumulated Digital Citizen Points and their corresponding rank (Active, Basic, or Unranked) based on their recorded activities in the system. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1 The Citizen has authenticated their identity, successfully logged into the system, and the session is still valid (UC-01). |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 The system successfully retrieves and displays the Citizen's point balance, rank, and a summary of their current incentive benefits. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The Citizen selects the "Inquire point and rank" feature on the application interface. The system verifies the validity of the current login session.  The system retrieves the Citizen's cached point balance, current rank, and recent point history from the database. The system displays the total points, rank, point transaction history, and a list of incentive policies corresponding to the Citizen's rank on the screen. The system displays the total points, rank, and a list of incentive policies corresponding to the Citizen's rank on the screen. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1 Citizen account is suspended: The system displays the current point balance but shows a prominent "Suspended" status banner, the reason for suspension (e.g., policy violation), and the expected resume date (if applicable). |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1 Data retrieval error: The system cannot connect to the database to retrieve point information. The system automatically logs the error and displays the message "Unable to load point data at this time. Please try again later." to the user. |  |  |
| Priority: | High, Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | High — Accessed frequently when citizens want to track their point progress and incentive benefits. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01 The highest rank (e.g., Active Digital Citizen) is determined by the configured maximum tier threshold (e.g., ≥ 350 points). BR-02 The intermediate rank (e.g., Basic Digital Citizen) is determined by the configured mid-tier threshold (e.g., 100 – 349 points). BR-03 The lowest status (e.g., Unranked) is determined by the configured minimum tier threshold (e.g., \< 100 points). |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: UC-01 Downstream: None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1 The Calculation Engine updates the citizen's point data instantaneously or in near real-time right after a valid activity is recorded. |  |  |

UC-03: Receive System Notifications

| UC ID and Name: | UC-03: Receive System Notifications |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen The Son | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | Citizen | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | The system records a change in the Citizen's point balance, rank, or incentive eligibility (e.g., triggered by the automated point calculation system |  |  |
| Description: | The system automatically sends real-time push notifications to the Citizen regarding important updates to their Digital Citizen Point account. This includes points awarded for completing digital activities, rank upgrades or downgrades, and notifications about new available incentives. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1 The Citizen has installed the application, successfully logged in at least once, and granted permission to receive push notifications on their mobile device. PRE-2 A system event (such as point addition, deduction, or rank evaluation) has successfully occurred and requires user notification. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 The notification is successfully delivered to the Citizen's device. POST-2 The notification message is safely stored in the Citizen's in-app notification inbox for future reference. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The system detects a triggering event (e.g., receiving \+7 points for completing an online public service). The system generates the notification content based on predefined communication templates. The system pushes the real-time notification to the Citizen's registered mobile device.  The system simultaneously saves a copy of the message in the Citizen's in-app notification history.  The Citizen receives and views the push notification on their device screen. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1 Citizen interacts with the notification: At step 5, the Citizen taps on the push notification. The system uses a Deep Link to automatically open the application and redirect the Citizen to the exact relevant detailed screen (e.g., tapping a point addition notification opens the Point History screen directly). |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1 Device offline or push notifications disabled: The system cannot deliver the push notification to the device. The system gracefully skips the push action but still completes Step 4 (saving the message in the in-app inbox) so the Citizen can read it upon their next login. |  |  |
| Priority: | Medium – Should Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Medium to High — Depends on how frequently the citizen participates in digital activities and system updates. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01 Notifications must clearly and transparently state the exact reason for any point changes (e.g., "+10 points for contributing to legal drafts"). BR-02 System notifications must comply with personal data protection regulations and must not expose sensitive personal information on the device's lock screen. BR-03 Notification Aggregation: If multiple events occur within a short window (e.g., 5 minutes), the system may aggregate notifications into a single summary message to avoid spamming the user. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: All Use Cases that trigger point, rank, or status changes (e.g., UC-16, UC-17, UC-20, UC-22). Downstream: UC-02 (Inquire Point Balance and Rank) |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1 The underlying push notification delivery service (e.g., Firebase Cloud Messaging) is fully operational and has low latency. |  |  |

UC-04: Submit Feedback and Complaint

| UC ID and Name: | UC-04: Submit Feedback and Complaint |  |  |
| ----- | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen The Son | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | Citizen  | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | The Citizen detects an error in the digital citizen point calculation or wishes to submit a recommendation, complaint, or report regarding system issues and digital rights. |  |  |
| Description: | This use case allows Citizens to submit feedback and complaints online via the VNeID platform. The system records the submission, issues a Tracking ID for competent authorities to process, and supports awarding incentive points for constructive recommendations. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1 The Citizen has a valid, active session on the system (having completed UC-01). |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 The complaint/feedback is securely saved in the database with an initial "Pending" status and a unique Tracking ID. This status will subsequently transition to "In Progress" and "Resolved/Rejected" throughout its lifecycle. POST-2 The system sends a confirmation notification to the Citizen's device (via UC-03). |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The Citizen selects the "Submit Feedback/Complaint" function on the application interface.  The system displays the data entry form. The Citizen fills in the required fields (Issue Category, Title, Detailed Description) and uploads supporting attachment files (if any).  The Citizen clicks the "Submit" button. The system validates the input data (checks for mandatory fields, valid file formats/sizes), performs a background virus scan on the attachments, and checks the anti-spam limit (per BR-02). The system saves the complaint record in the database with a "Pending" status and automatically generates a Tracking ID. The system displays a success screen showing the Tracking ID. The system triggers UC-03 to send a push notification confirming the receipt of the submission. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1 Cancel Operation: At step 3 or 4, the Citizen clicks "Cancel". The system clears all temporarily entered data from the form and returns to the previous screen. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1 Invalid Data: At step 5, if mandatory information is missing, the system highlights the missing fields in red and requests the Citizen to provide the data. EX-2 Attachment Error: At step 5, if an attached file exceeds the allowed size or has an invalid format (per BR-01), the system rejects the upload and displays a specific error message. EX-3 Complaint Limit Exceeded: At step 5, if the Citizen violates rule  EX-4 Virus Detected: At step 5, if the virus scan detects a security threat in an attachment, the system rejects the entire request, logs a security alert, and displays an upload error message to the citizen. |  |  |
| Priority: | Medium, Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Low — Typically occurs only when there are system errors, point discrepancies, or when a citizen wishes to provide constructive feedback. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01 Supporting attachment files must not exceed 10MB per file and must be in standard formats: PDF, JPG, PNG, or DOCX. BR-02 To prevent spam, each Citizen is allowed a maximum of 3 complaints/feedback submissions in the "Pending" status at any given time. BR-03 Points are not awarded for general system error complaints. Only when an Admin marks a submission as "Successfully Processed" (Resolved) AND explicitly flags it as "Constructive Feedback" will the system automatically trigger UC-16 to award the citizen 5 points. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: UC-01 (Authenticate User) Downstream: UC-03 (Receive System Notifications), UC-07 (Resolve Complaints), UC-16 (Calculate and Add Points) |  |  |
|         Assumptions: | AS-1 The Storage Service handling attachment uploads is stable, has sufficient capacity, and is integrated with an effective Antivirus API. |  |  |

UC-05 – Configure Scoring Rules

| UC ID and Name: | UC-05:  Configure Scoring Rules |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen The Son | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | System Administrator | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | The System Administrator needs to add, update, or deactivate a point calculation rule and retention rate for the system. |  |  |
| Description: | This use case allows the Administrator to set the core parameters for the Point Calculation Engine. The parameters include: Activity Name, Activity Category, Point Value, and Frequency Limit. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1 The Administrator has successfully logged into the Admin Portal via 2FA (completed UC-01) and holds the system configuration management role. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 The new or updated scoring rule is successfully saved in the database with strict version control. POST-2 The Calculation Engine (UC-16) automatically applies the new rule for transactions occurring after the configuration is approved. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The Administrator selects the "Scoring Rules Management" module on the Admin Portal navigation bar. The system displays a list of activity categories (e.g., Data Management, Digital Service Usage, Community Participation) and the current active rules. The Administrator selects "Add New Rule" (or selects an existing rule to "Edit"). The system displays the rule configuration form. The Administrator enters the mandatory parameters: Activity Name, Category, Point Value (e.g., 7 points), **and Frequency** Limit (e.g., per week/month/year or a one-time occurrence).  The Administrator clicks "Submit for Approval".  The system validates the input data (checking for positive integers, ensuring activity codes are not duplicated) and saves the configuration with a "Pending Approval" status. A Super Admin logs in, reviews the pending configuration, and clicks "Approve". The system creates a new version (Valid From today), expires the old version (setting a "Valid To" date), and automatically clears the cache to apply it immediately.automatically clears the cache to apply it immediately. The system displays a "Scoring rule approved and applied successfully" message. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1 Deactivate Rule: At step 3, the Administrator selects an active rule and changes its status to "Inactive". This action also follows the Maker-Checker approval flow. Subsequent activities will no longer accrue points under this rule. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1 Invalid Data: At step 7, if the entered point value or retention rate is not a valid number (e.g., entering letters or negative numbers), the system blocks the save action and highlights the erroneous field in red. EX-2 Duplicate Rule: At step 7, if the Administrator creates a rule with the same "Activity Code" as an existing one, the system reports a duplication error and requests a code change. |  |  |
| Priority: | High, Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Low — Used only during initial system setup or when legal documents alter the point policies. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01 The configured point value must be a positive integer. The point system does not apply negative point rules (it is not used for penalization). BR-02 "Hard deleting" a rule that has already been used to generate points for a citizen is strictly prohibited. It can only be "Soft deleted" (deactivated) to preserve the integrity of the system's ledger. BR-03 Rules eligible for retention (such as completing administrative procedures, contributing to drafts) can only be configured with the maximum allowed retention rate (e.g., 30% carried over to the next year). BR-04 Versioning: Existing rules cannot be directly modified if they have already been used. Modifying an active rule creates a new version with a specified "Valid From" date, while the old version is given a "Valid To" date and preserved for audit history. BR-05 Maker-Checker: Any addition or modification to scoring rules requires dual authorization. The initiating Admin (Maker) submits the rule, and a Super Admin (Checker) must approve it before it becomes active. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: UC-01 (Authenticate User) Downstream: UC-16 (Calculate and Add Points \- Consumes data from this Use Case). |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1 When the configuration is saved, the system automatically handles cache synchronization (e.g., Redis/Memcached) across all server instances to ensure immediate consistency in point calculations. |  |  |

UC-06: Configure Tiered Incentives

| UC ID and Name: | UC-06 – Configure Tiered Incentives |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen The Son | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | System Administrator,Super Admin | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | The Administrator needs to update the point thresholds for citizen ranking (Active, Basic, Unranked) or adjust the tax, fee, and charge reduction rates according to new Government regulations. |  |  |
| Description: | This use case allows the Administrator to set up and manage the point thresholds for digital citizen rankings. Additionally, it enables mapping specific incentive policies (e.g., a 10% reduction in registration fees, fee exemptions for administrative procedures) to the corresponding citizen ranks. This data acts as the source of truth for external satellite systems (Tax Systems, Public Service Portals) to apply benefits when citizens perform transactions. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1 The Administrator has successfully authenticated via the Admin Portal (completed UC-01) and holds the incentive policy management role. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 The updated rank point thresholds or incentive mappings are securely saved in the database with a strict versioning system. POST-2 The system updates its API endpoints (consumed by external systems) to reflect the latest incentive policies upon approval. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The Administrator selects the "Tiered Incentives Management" module on the Admin Portal. The system displays the current point threshold configuration for all ranks: Active, Basic, and Unranked Digital Citizens. The system displays the list of incentive policies currently mapped to each rank. The Administrator selects "Edit Point Thresholds" or "Add/Edit Incentive Policy". The Administrator enters the new parameters. For example: Setting a "10% reduction in vehicle registration fee" incentive and mapping it to the "Active Digital Citizen" rank. The Administrator clicks "Submit for Approval". The system validates the input data (ensuring point thresholds do not overlap and the incentive percentage is between 0-100%) and saves the changes with a "Pending Approval" status. A Super Admin logs in, reviews the pending configuration, and clicks "Approve". The system creates a new configuration version (with a 'Valid From' timestamp), expires the old version, and automatically clears the cache. The system logs the entire configuration action and displays a "Policy approved and updated successfully" message. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1 Deactivate an Incentive: At step 4, the Administrator selects an existing incentive policy and changes its status to "Inactive". This action also requires the Maker-Checker approval flow. Transactions occurring after approval will no longer receive that specific benefit. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1 Threshold Overlap Error: At step 7, if the Administrator configures the Basic rank as 100-350 and the Active rank as ≥ 300, the system detects the overlap, blocks the save operation, and displays an error prompting the user to adjust the thresholds. EX-2 Invalid Percentage Rate: At step 7, if the fee reduction rate entered is \> 100% or \< 0%, the system flags a data format error and refuses to save. |  |  |
| Priority: | High, Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Low — Executed only when there are changes to tax or fee incentive policies mandated by amended Government Appendices. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01 Rank thresholds must be continuous and mutually exclusive to ensure a citizen belongs to only one specific rank at any given time. BR-02 Any changes to incentive configurations apply strictly to future transactions (occurring after the configuration is saved) and must never be applied retroactively to alter past transaction history. BR-03 The incentive policies available to a citizen are strictly bound to their current rank, as determined by the dynamically configured point thresholds. The highest tier (e.g., Active) receives the maximum configured benefits, while lower tiers receive a defined subset.  BR-04 Maker-Checker Principle: Any modifications to rank thresholds or tax reduction rates must be submitted as a 'Pending Approval' request by an Initiating Admin. The changes only take effect after being reviewed and approved by a distinct Super Admin. BR-05 Configuration Versioning: To enforce BR-02 (no retroactivity), threshold and incentive configurations are never hard-updated. Saving an approved configuration creates a new version with a 'Valid From' timestamp, while expiring the previous version (setting a 'Valid To' date) to preserve the audit trail for past transactions.  |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: UC-01 (Authenticate User). Downstream: External integrated systems (Tax system, National Public Service Portal). |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1 External service-providing systems (like the General Department of Taxation's application) will automatically call the Digital Citizen Point System's API to query the citizen's rank and incentive rate at the time of payment processing. |  |  |

UC-07a–Resolve Complaints

| UC ID and Name: | UC-07a–Resolve Complaints |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen The Son | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | System Administrator | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | The Administrator receives a notification of a new "Pending" complaint from a citizen. |  |  |
| Description: | This use case encompasses receiving, verifying, and resolving citizen complaints and feedback regarding point calculations (from UC-04). |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1 The Administrator has successfully logged into the Admin Portal (UC-01) and holds the "Complaint Resolution" role. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 The status of the complaint is updated to "Resolved" or "Rejected" along with an official response. POST-2 The system triggers UC-03 to notify the citizen and may trigger UC-16 if points are awarded. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The Administrator accesses the "Complaint Management" module. The system displays a list of complaints currently in the "Pending" status. The Administrator selects a record to view its detailed content, attached evidence, and the citizen's point history. The Administrator conducts data cross-checking and verification. The Administrator updates the record status to "Resolved" or "Rejected" and enters a detailed response/resolution reason. The system saves the processing result and action history into the database. The system triggers UC-03 to send a push notification with the result to the citizen. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1 Escalate/Forward Complaint: At step 5, if the issue is complex, the Administrator can change the status to "Under Investigation" or assign it to another department. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1 Missing Resolution Reason: At step 5, if the Administrator selects "Rejected" but leaves the reason field blank, the system blocks the action and requires a rejection reason to be entered. |  |  |
| Priority: | Medium — Complaint handling occurs daily/weekly; Report viewing occurs periodically (weekly/monthly/quarterly). |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Daily/Weekly |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01 Any complaint resolution decision (Resolved/Rejected) must include a "Reason/Feedback" entry to ensure transparency and maintain a reliable audit trail. BR-02 If the complaint is categorized as "Reflection/Recommendation to State Agencies" and is marked as "Successfully Processed" by the Admin, the system automatically triggers UC-16 to award the configured point value for constructive feedback. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: UC-04 (Submit Feedback and Complaint). Downstream: UC-03 (Receive System Notifications) |  |  |
| Assumptions: | None |  |  |

UC-07b – View Analytics Reports

| UC ID and Name: | UC-07b – View Analytics Reports |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen The Son | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | System Administrator  | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | There is a management request to extract periodic statistical reports. |  |  |
| Description: | Querying and generating analytical reports on digital citizen rank distribution, incentive usage, and overall system status. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1 The Administrator has successfully logged into the Admin Portal (UC-01) and holds the "Report Access" role. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 Statistical reports are successfully generated and exported as charts or data files. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The Administrator accesses the "Reports & Analytics" module. The Administrator selects the report type (e.g., Citizen Rank Distribution, Service Usage Frequency) and configures the time filter. The system queries the database and displays the results in visual charts/data tables. The Administrator clicks the "Export" button to download the file in Excel or PDF format. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | None |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1 Report Query Timeout: At step 3, if the queried date range is too large causing data overload, the system automatically times out, displays an error, and prompts the Administrator to narrow the date range. |  |  |
| Priority: | Medium |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Weekly/monthly/quarterly |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01 Data within the reporting module must have all sensitive Personal Identifiable Information (PII) anonymized, displaying only aggregated data. |  |  |
| Other Information: | None |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1 The reporting system fetches data from a Data Warehouse (or a Read-Replica Database) synchronized periodically, ensuring heavy analytical queries do not degrade the performance of the main transactional database (OLTP). |  |  |

UC-08: Record Profile Completion  
**Functional Description Template**

| UC ID and Name: | UC-08 – Record Profile Completion |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Truong Hong Quy | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | VNeID Core Identity System | Secondary Actors: | Citizen |
| Trigger: | A citizen successfully completes their digital identity profile in the VNeID application. |  |  |
| Description: | The system acts as a data ingestion endpoint that receives a record from VNeID Core when a citizen records 100% completion of the VNeID base profile. The system verifies the data origin, checks for existing records to prevent duplication, securely logs the event with a specific state (Valid, Duplicate, or Rejected), and forwards it to the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16). |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1: The citizen account exists in the VCP system database. PRE-2: The profile completion event is verified by VNeID Core |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1: The profile completion event is recorded in the activity log with an appropriate status (Valid, Rejected, or Duplicate). POST-2: If valid, the information is forwarded to the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16). |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | 1\. VNeID Core sends a profile completion record to the VCP system. 2\. The system verifies the validity and origin of the data to ensure the event genuinely comes from the authorized VNeID Core system. 3\. The system authenticates the citizen identity against the citizen database. 4\. The system checks the activity log to ensure this specific citizen has not previously recorded a valid profile completion event. 5\. The system records the event in the activity log with a "Valid" status. 6\. The system successfully records the event and forwards the information to the point calculation engine (UC-16) to process the ranking. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1: Citizen identity is not found. The system rejects the record, logs the event with a "Rejected \- Not Found" status, and notifies the external system of the mismatch. AF-2: Citizen account is currently suspended. The system records the event with a "Rejected \- Suspended" status. The event is not forwarded to UC-16. AF-3: Duplicate completion detected. The system records the event with a "Duplicate" status and ends the process without triggering UC-16. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1: Network connection or data origin verification fails. The system rejects the record securely and logs a high-priority integration alert for the administrator. |  |  |
| Priority: | High \- Must Have  |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Low (typically a one-time event per citizen). |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01: This specific activity is recorded and eligible for reward only once per citizen's lifetime. BR-02: Suspended citizens cannot have valid activity logs recorded for points. BR-03: The point value awarded and the retention rules applied for this activity are determined dynamically by the configured scoring rules (UC-05) and executed entirely by the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16). |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: VNeID Core system. Downstream: UC-16 – Calculate and Add Points. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1: Profile data sent by VNeID Core is authentic and pre-validated. |  |  |

UC-09: Record Document Integration  
**Functional Description Template**

| UC ID and Name: | UC-09 – Record Document Integration |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Truong Hong Quy | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | VNeID Core Identity System | Secondary Actors: | Citizen |
| Trigger: | A citizen successfully integrates an official document (e.g., driver's license, health insurance) into VNeID. |  |  |
| Description: | The system receives a record indicating the successful digital integration of official cards. The system verifies the origin of the data, validates the entry to ensure the specific document type has not been integrated before by the citizen, logs the event with a specific status, and forwards it to the point calculation module. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1: Citizen account exists in the VCP system. PRE-2: Document integration is successfully completed and verified by VNeID Core. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1: The specific document integration event is stored in the activity log with a status (Valid, Duplicate, or Rejected). POST-2: If valid, the event is forwarded to UC-16. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | 1\. VNeID Core sends a document integration record (including document type and timestamp) to the VCP system. 2\. The system verifies the validity and origin of the data to ensure it genuinely comes from the authorized VNeID Core system. 3\. The system authenticates the citizen identity against the citizen database. 4\. The system checks the activity log to verify if this exact document type has already been integrated by this specific citizen. 5\. The system confirms the citizen's account is active (not suspended). 6\. The system records the integration event in the activity log with a "Valid" status. 7\. The system successfully records the event and forwards the information to the point calculation engine (UC-16) to process the reward. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1: Citizen identity is not found. The system rejects the record and logs a "Rejected \- Not Found" status. AF-2: Citizen is suspended. The system records the event with a "Rejected \- Suspended" status and ends the flow. AF-3: Duplicate document type detected. The system records the event with a "Duplicate" status and ends the flow. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1: Unrecognized document type code in the record. The system rejects the record, logs a "Rejected \- Invalid Data" status, and fires an exception alert. EX-2: Data origin verification fails (potential spoofing). The system drops the record immediately and logs a high-priority security alert. |  |  |
| Priority: | High, Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Medium – occurs whenever a citizen integrates a new official document into VNeID |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01: A citizen can only earn points once per specific document type (e.g., integrating a B2 Driver's License twice will only be considered valid the first time). BR-02: The document categories must strictly match the standardized list governed by the system's scoring configurations. BR-03: The system strictly separates data ingestion from point logic; the actual point value awarded is determined dynamically by the configured rules (UC-05) and executed entirely by the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16). |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: VNeID Core system. Downstream: UC-16 – Calculate and Add Points. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1: All documents integrated into VNeID are authentic, legally valid, and pre-verified by the national identity database before the record is sent to the VCP system. |  |  |

UC-10: Record App Engagement  
**Functional Description Template**

| UC ID and Name: | UC-10 – Record App Engagement |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Truong Hong Quy | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | VNeID Core Identity System | Secondary Actors: | Citizen |
| Trigger: | Citizen logs into or interacts with the VNeID application. |  |  |
| Description: | The system acts as a data ingestion endpoint that receives periodic usage frequency logs from VNeID Core to determine digital engagement levels. The system verifies the data origin, applies a rate-limiting check to filter out spam or artificial engagement, securely logs the event with a specific audit status (Valid or Rejected), and forwards valid events to the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16). |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1: The citizen account exists in the VCP system database . PRE-2: The user login or active session event is securely authenticated and authorized by VNeID Core. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1: The engagement activity is securely recorded in the activity log with an appropriate status (Valid or Rejected) . POST-2: If valid, the event information is forwarded to UC-16 for point calculation. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | 1\. VNeID Core sends an engagement record (active session log) to the VCP system. 2\. The system verifies the validity and origin of the data to ensure it genuinely comes from the authorized VNeID Core system. 3\. The system authenticates the citizen identity against the citizen database. 4\. The system checks the activity log to calculate the time elapsed since the last recorded engagement event for this specific citizen to prevent spam (Rate-Limiting Check). 5\. The system confirms the time elapsed exceeds the minimum required interval for engagement. 6\. The system confirms the citizen's account is active (not suspended). 7\. The system records the engagement event in the activity log with a "Valid" status. 8\. The system successfully records the event and forwards the information to the point calculation engine (UC-16) to process the reward. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1: Citizen identity is not found at Step 3\. The system rejects the record, logs a "Rejected \- Not Found" status, and notifies VNeID Core of the mismatch . AF-2: Rate limit exceeded (Spam detected) at Step 5\. If the time elapsed is below the allowed threshold, the system records the event with a "Rejected \- Rate Limit Exceeded" status. The flow ends without triggering UC-16 . AF-3: Citizen is suspended at Step 6\. The system records the event with a "Rejected \- Suspended" status and ends the flow. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1: Data origin verification fails (potential spoofing). The system drops the record immediately and logs a high-priority security alert. EX-2: System connection timeout due to high concurrent traffic. The system relies on VNeID Core's retry mechanism to process the event later. |  |  |
| Priority: | High – Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Very High (triggered daily/weekly by millions of active citizens). |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01: The system must strictly apply rate-limiting to incoming engagement logs to prevent point farming via automated login scripts . BR-02: The system strictly separates data ingestion from point logic; the application of point values, tracking of the weekly frequency limit, and year-end carryover logic are deferred entirely to the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16) based on configurations from UC-05. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: VNeID Core system. Downstream: UC-16 – Calculate and Add Points. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1: Login and active session data authenticated by VNeID Core accurately represents legitimate citizen engagement. |  |  |

UC-11: Record Public Service Completion  
**Functional Description Template**

| UC ID and Name: | UC-11: Record Public Service Completion |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Truong Hong Quy | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | Public Service Platform (DVC Portal) | Secondary Actors: | Citizen |
| Trigger: | Citizen successfully completes an online administrative procedure (Level 4\) on the National or Provincial Public Service Portal. |  |  |
| Description: | The system acts as a data ingestion endpoint that receives verified transaction data from government Public Service Platforms indicating the successful execution of an online administrative procedure. The system verifies the data origin, guarantees the idempotency of the transaction to prevent double-counting, securely logs the event with a specific audit status (Valid, Duplicate, or Rejected), and forwards valid events to the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16). |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1: The citizen account exists in the VCP system database. PRE-2: The citizen has successfully executed a digital service. PRE-3: The Public Service Platform has officially verified and authorized the completion of the procedure. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1: The public service completion event is securely recorded in the activity log with an appropriate status (Valid, Duplicate, or Rejected). POST-2: If valid, the event information is forwarded to UC-16 for point calculation. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | 1\. The Public Service Platform sends a transaction completion record (including transaction reference and service code) to the VCP system. 2\. The system verifies the validity and origin of the data to ensure it genuinely comes from the authorized Public Service Platform. 3\. The system authenticates the citizen identity against the citizen database. 4\. The system checks the activity log using the unique transaction reference to ensure idempotency (i.e., this specific transaction has not been processed before). 5\. The system confirms the citizen's account is active (not suspended). 6\. The system records the public service event in the activity log with a "Valid" status. 7\. The system successfully records the event and forwards the information to the point calculation engine (UC-16) to process the reward. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1: Citizen identity is not found at Step 3\. The system rejects the record, logs a "Rejected \- Not Found" status, and notifies the Public Service Platform of the mismatch. AF-2: Duplicate transaction reference detected at Step 4\. The system records the event with a "Duplicate" status and ends the flow without triggering UC-16. AF-3: Citizen is suspended at Step 5\. The system records the event with a "Rejected \- Suspended" status and ends the flow. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1: Data origin verification fails. The system drops the record securely and logs a high-priority integration error. EX-2: System connection timeout. The system relies on the Public Service Platform's retry mechanism to resend the record later. |  |  |
| Priority: | High – Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Medium |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01: The activity must be a fully executed and verified digital service. BR-02: Each unique transaction reference provided by the Public Service Platform is strictly recorded only once to guarantee idempotency. BR-03: The system strictly separates data ingestion from point logic; the application of point values (e.g., for executing online administrative procedures) and year-end carryover logic are deferred entirely to the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16) based on configurations from UC-05. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: DVC Portal. Downstream: UC-16 – Calculate and Add Points. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1: Public Service Platforms provide accurate, non-repudiable, and fully verified completion data before sending it to the VCP system. |  |  |

UC-12: Record Financial Transactions  
**Functional Description Template**

| UC ID and Name: | UC-12: Record Financial Transactions |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Truong Hong Quy | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | Financial Partner (Banks, E-wallets, E-commerce Platforms) | Secondary Actors: | Citizen |
| Trigger: | A citizen successfully completes a digital financial transaction, shopping, or e-commerce activity authenticated via VNeID. |  |  |
| Description: | The system acts as a data ingestion endpoint that receives verified transaction records from integrated Financial Partners. The system verifies the partner's authorization and data origin, ensures transaction idempotency to prevent double-counting, securely logs the financial activity with a specific audit status (Valid, Duplicate, or Rejected), and forwards valid events to the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16). |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1: The citizen account exists in the VCP system database. PRE-2: A digital financial or e-commerce transaction has been successfully executed. PRE-3: The transaction was strictly authenticated using VNeID identity verification. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1: The transaction data is securely recorded in the activity log with an appropriate status (Valid, Duplicate, or Rejected). POST-2: If valid, the event information is forwarded to UC-16 for point calculation. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | 1\. The Financial Partner sends a transaction record (including transaction reference, partner ID, and activity type) to the VCP system. 2\. The system verifies the validity and origin of the data to ensure it genuinely comes from the authorized Financial Partner. 3\. The system authenticates the citizen identity against the citizen database. 4\. The system checks the activity log using the unique transaction reference and partner ID to ensure idempotency (i.e., this exact transaction has not been processed before). 5\. The system verifies that the activity type maps to a predefined scoring category in the system configurations. 6\. The system confirms the citizen's account is active (not suspended). 7\. The system records the valid transaction event in the activity log with a "Valid" status. 8\. The system successfully records the event and forwards the information to the point calculation engine (UC-16) to process the reward. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1: Citizen identity is not found at Step 3\. The system rejects the record, logs a "Rejected \- Not Found" status, and notifies the Financial Partner of the mismatch. AF-2: Duplicate transaction reference detected at Step 4\. The system records the event with a "Duplicate" status and ends the flow without triggering UC-16. AF-3: Unrecognized activity type at Step 5\. The system records the event with a "Rejected \- Unmapped Activity" status and ends the flow. AF-4: Citizen is suspended at Step 6\. The system records the event with a "Rejected \- Suspended" status and ends the flow. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1: Data origin verification fails (potential spoofing). The system drops the record immediately and logs a critical security alert. EX-2: System connection timeout or forwarding failure. The system records the event in the activity log and relies on a background reconciliation job or the partner's retry mechanism to process the event later. |  |  |
| Priority: | High – Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | High (processed continuously as citizens make daily transactions). |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01: Digital financial transactions and e-commerce services must carry a valid VNeID authentication trace; transactions lacking this trace are structurally rejected. BR-02: Each transaction must possess a globally unique transaction reference provided by the partner to guarantee idempotency and prevent point farming. BR-03: The system strictly separates data ingestion from point logic; the assignment of actual point values and enforcement of any related caps or tier logic are deferred entirely to the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16) based on configurations from UC-05. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: Financial Partner. Downstream: UC-16 – Calculate and Add Points. |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1: Financial partners implement robust internal anti-fraud mechanisms and provide reliable, securely authenticated transaction logs before sending data to the VCP system. |  |  |

UC-13: Record Healthcare and Donation  
**Functional Description Template**

| UC ID and Name: | UC-13: Record Healthcare and Donation |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Truong Hong Quy | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | Healthcare Platform | Secondary Actors: | Citizen |
| Trigger: | A citizen successfully uses a digital healthcare service (e.g., booking an online appointment, receiving digital test results) or registers a voluntary blood donation via an integrated health platform.  |  |  |
| Description: | The system acts as a data ingestion endpoint that receives verified healthcare interaction data from the National or Provincial Healthcare Platforms. The system ensures strict patient data privacy by extracting only non-sensitive metadata for processing, verifies the data origin, guarantees transaction idempotency to prevent double-counting, securely logs the event with a specific audit status (Valid, Duplicate, or Rejected), and forwards valid events to the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16).  |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1: The citizen account exists in the VCP system database. PRE-2: The citizen has completed an interaction with an integrated digital healthcare service or donated blood. PRE-3: The Healthcare Platform has officially verified and authorized the interaction event.  |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1: The healthcare/donation activity is securely logged in the activity log with an appropriate status (Valid, Duplicate, or Rejected). POST-2: If valid, the event information is forwarded to UC-16 for point calculation. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | 1\. The Healthcare Platform sends an interaction record (metadata including event reference, partner ID, and activity type) to the VCP system. 2\. The system verifies the validity and origin of the data to ensure it genuinely comes from the authorized Healthcare Platform. 3\. The system authenticates the citizen identity against the citizen database. 4\. The system checks the activity log using the unique event reference and partner ID to ensure idempotency (i.e., this exact medical booking or donation has not been processed before). 5\. The system verifies that the activity type maps to a predefined scoring category (e.g., "MEDICAL\_BOOKING" or "BLOOD\_DONATION") in the system configurations. 6\. The system confirms the citizen's account is active (not suspended). 7\. The system records the valid event in the activity log with a "Valid" status. 8\. The system successfully records the event and forwards the information to the point calculation engine (UC-16) to process the reward. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1: Citizen identity is not found at Step 3\. The system rejects the record, logs a "Rejected \- Not Found" status, and notifies the Healthcare Platform of the mismatch. AF-2: Duplicate event reference detected at Step 4\. The system records the event with a "Duplicate" status and ends the flow without triggering UC-16. AF-3: Unrecognized activity type at Step 5\. The system records the event with a "Rejected \- Unmapped Activity" status and ends the flow. AF-4: Citizen is suspended at Step 6\. The system records the event with a "Rejected \- Suspended" status and ends the flow. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1: Data origin verification fails (potential spoofing). The system drops the record immediately and logs a high-priority security alert. EX-2: System connection timeout or forwarding failure. The system records the event in the activity log and relies on a background reconciliation job or the platform's retry mechanism to process the event later. |  |  |
| Priority: | Medium – Should Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Low to Medium (triggered when citizens interact with digital medical services or participate in blood donation drives). |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01: **Strict Data Anonymization & Privacy Rule:** The VCP system must only process and store event metadata (event reference, activity type, timestamp). It must strictly reject and NEVER store any actual medical diagnoses, test outcomes, or sensitive Personal Health Information (PHI). BR-02: Each unique event reference provided by the Healthcare platform is strictly recorded only once to guarantee idempotency. BR-03: The system strictly separates data ingestion from point logic; the assignment of actual point values (e.g., for booking appointments or voluntary blood donations) and enforcement of any related rules are deferred entirely to the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16) based on configurations from UC-05 |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: Healthcare Platform / Electronic Health Book System. Downstream: UC-16 (Calculate and Add Points). |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1: Healthcare systems provide verified medical data and handle all underlying patient consent and privacy regulations (like HIPAA or local equivalents) before sharing the integration metadata with the VCP system.  |  |  |

UC-14: Record Digital Literacy Completion

| UC ID and Name: | UC-14: View Civic Point History |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Truong Hong Quy | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | Education Platform | Secondary Actors: | Citizen |
| Trigger: | A citizen successfully completes a recognized digital literacy or online safety course and is issued a digital certificate. |  |  |
| Description: | The system acts as a data ingestion endpoint that receives certification records from state-approved educational platforms. The system verifies the data origin, validates the certificate to ensure the citizen hasn't already been rewarded for the exact same course, securely logs the completion event with a specific audit status (Valid, Duplicate, or Rejected), and forwards valid events to the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16). |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1: The citizen account exists in the VCP system database. PRE-2: The citizen has successfully completed a qualified digital literacy course. PRE-3: The Education Platform has officially issued, verified, and authorized the certification record. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1: The certification record is securely logged in the activity log with an appropriate status (Valid, Duplicate, or Rejected). POST-2: If valid, the event information is forwarded to UC-16 for point calculation. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | 1\. The Education Platform sends a certification record (including certificate reference and course code) to the VCP system. 2\. The system verifies the validity and origin of the data to ensure it genuinely comes from the authorized Education Platform. 3\. The system authenticates the citizen identity against the citizen database. 4\. The system checks the activity log to verify that this specific certificate reference or course code has not been processed previously for this citizen (Idempotency check). 5\. The system verifies that the course code maps to a predefined, approved course in the system configurations. 6\. The system confirms the citizen's account is active (not suspended). 7\. The system records the valid completion event in the activity log with a "Valid" status. 8\. The system successfully records the event and forwards the information to the point calculation engine (UC-16) to process the reward. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1: Citizen identity is not found at Step 3\. The system rejects the record, logs a "Rejected \- Not Found" status, and notifies the Education Platform of the mismatch. AF-2: Duplicate course code or certificate reference detected at Step 4\. The system records the event with a "Duplicate" status and ends the flow without triggering UC-16. AF-3: Unrecognized course code at Step 5\. The system records the event with a "Rejected \- Unmapped Course" status and ends the flow. AF-4: Citizen is suspended at Step 6\. The system records the event with a "Rejected \- Suspended" status and ends the flow. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1: Data origin verification fails (potential spoofing). The system drops the record immediately and logs a high-priority security alert. EX-2: System connection timeout or forwarding failure. The system records the event in the activity log and relies on a background reconciliation job or the platform's retry mechanism to process the event later. |  |  |
| Priority: | Medium – Should Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Low (Citizens typically complete these courses once or infrequently). |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01: Only courses explicitly approved and mapped in the system's configurations are eligible for processing. BR-02: A citizen can only process a valid completion record once per specific course code. BR-03: The system strictly separates data ingestion from point logic; the assignment of actual point values (e.g., for completing a state-approved digital literacy course) is deferred entirely to the Point Calculation Engine (UC-16) based on configurations from UC-05. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream: Education Platform Downstream: UC-16 (Calculate and Add Points) |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1: Education platforms maintain reliable, tamper-proof certification records and handle all course completion validation before pushing data to the VCP system. |  |  |

UC-15 – Record Civic Engagement

| UC ID and Name: | UC-15 – Record Civic Engagement |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen Trung Anh | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | Legislative Platform | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | A citizen contributes to a legal draft, responds to a government survey, or interacts with a National Assembly deputy through the Legislative Platform. |  |  |
| Description: | Records civic participation activities submitted through the Legislative Platform, including contributions to legal drafts, responses to government policy surveys, and documented interactions with National Assembly deputies. The ingestion system validates the data format, checks for duplicates using the verification reference (document ID), and stores the record. Point calculation, cap checks, and account status checks are delegated entirely to UC-16. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1	The citizen exists in the VCP system. PRE-2	The civic participation has been verified by the Legislative Platform. PRE-3	A scoring rule exists for the specific civic engagement activity type (UC-05). |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 The civic engagement activity is recorded in the activity log, OR rejected with a reason. POST-2 If valid, the activity is forwarded to UC-16 for point calculation. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The Legislative Platform confirms that a citizen has participated in a civic activity (legal draft contribution, government survey, or National Assembly deputy interaction). The Legislative Platform sends the participation record to the VCP system, including: citizen identifier, activity type, activity date, verification reference (document ID or survey ID), and participation details. The system validates the citizen's identity and the completeness of the submitted data. The system checks whether this same participation has already been recorded (same citizen, same activity type, same verification reference). The system records the activity in the activity log and forwards it to UC-16 for point calculation. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1	Duplicate civic participation detected (same citizen, same activity type, same verification reference). 	The activity is marked as duplicate. No further processing occurs. The record is preserved for audit. AF-2	Citizen is not found in the VCP system. 	The activity is logged with a rejection reason. The Legislative Platform is notified of the mismatch for investigation. AF-3	The Legislative Platform sends a batch of multiple participation records at once. 	The system processes each record independently. A failure for one record does not affect the processing of others. AF-4	The participation record is missing required fields (e.g., activity date, verification reference). 	The system rejects the record with a validation error. The Legislative Platform is notified to correct and resend. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1	Network failure between Legislative Platform and VCP. 	The Legislative Platform retries. If retries fail, the event is queued for later processing. EX-2	System error during activity recording. 	The operation is cancelled and retried automatically. If retries fail, the Administrator is alerted. |  |  |
| Priority: | Medium – Should Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Low to Medium — depends on government consultation activity. Legal draft consultations occur periodically. Surveys and deputy interactions vary seasonally. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01	Each civic participation event is counted once per citizen per specific draft, survey, or interaction (identified by the verification reference or document ID). A citizen contributing to the same legal draft twice is a duplicate, but contributing to two different drafts on the same day is two valid activities. BR-02	Point values for each civic engagement activity type are determined by the configured scoring rules (UC-05). For example, legal draft contributions may earn \+10 points per contribution. BR-03	Only participation records verified by the Legislative Platform are accepted. Self-reported civic engagement is not supported. BR-04	Civic engagement activities are subject to the same frequency limits, category caps, and overall annual caps as all other activity types (enforced by UC-16, not by this ingestion use case). |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream	Legislative Platform (participation verification) Downstream	UC-16 (Calculate and Add Points) |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1	The Legislative Platform accurately verifies and reports citizen participation in civic activities. AS-2	The Legislative Platform provides records in the agreed data format documented in the integration specification. AS-3	Every civic engagement record includes a unique verification reference (document ID or survey ID) that distinguishes different drafts, surveys, or interactions.  |  |  |

UC-16 – Calculate and Add Points

| UC ID and Name: | UC-16 – Calculate and Add Points |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen Trung Anh | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | System (automated) | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | A verified activity is recorded by any ingestion use case (UC-08 through UC-15). |  |  |
| Description: | The core point calculation engine. When a verified activity is recorded, the system looks up the scoring rule, validates the activity against business constraints (duplicate, frequency limit, category cap, annual cap, suspension), calculates the point value (full or partial), records the points, updates the citizen's balance, and triggers rank evaluation. Points are attributed to the fiscal year of the activity date. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1	The activity has been verified and recorded by an ingestion use case. PRE-2	The citizen has an active account in the system. PRE-3	A valid scoring rule exists for this activity type. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1	Points are recorded in the citizen's ledger and their balance is updated, OR the activity is rejected with a recorded reason. POST-2	The citizen's rank is re-evaluated (UC-17) and a notification is sent (UC-03). |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The system receives a verified activity record. The system identifies the fiscal year from the activity date. The system confirms the citizen's account is active. The system looks up the scoring rule for this activity type. The system checks that this activity has not already been counted (duplicate check).
