The system checks that the citizen has not exceeded the frequency limit for this activity type this year. The system checks that the citizen has not exceeded the category annual cap. The system checks that the citizen has not exceeded the overall annual cap. The system calculates the point value. If a cap is partially reached, only the remaining points are awarded. The system records the points in the ledger and updates the citizen's balance. The system triggers rank evaluation (UC-17) and sends a notification (UC-03). |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1	Citizen account is suspended. 	\+Activity is logged but no points are awarded. AF-2	No matching scoring rule found. 	\+Activity is logged with a warning. No points are awarded. AF-3	Duplicate activity detected. 	\+Marked as duplicate. No points are awarded. AF-4	Frequency limit or cap fully reached.	             \+Activity is logged with the reason. No points are awarded. AF-5	Cap is partially reached. 	\+Only the remaining points up to the cap are awarded. Citizen is notified. AF-6	Activity occurred in a previous fiscal year but processed now.	\+Points are assigned to the fiscal year of the activity date. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1	System error during point recording. 	Operation is cancelled and retried automatically. If retries fail, administrator is alerted. EX-2	Point balance becomes inconsistent with ledger. 	System automatically recalculates the balance from ledger records. |  |  |
| Priority: | High – Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Very High — triggered for every verified citizen activity across all 8 ingestion categories (UC-08 through UC-15). |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01	Each activity is counted only once per citizen per date. BR-02	Each activity type has a maximum frequency per fiscal year. BR-03	Each activity category has an annual point cap. BR-04	Each citizen has an overall annual point cap. BR-05	When a cap is partially reached, remaining points are awarded rather than rejecting the entire activity. BR-06	Suspended citizens cannot earn points. BR-07	Points belong to the fiscal year of the activity date, not the processing date. BR-08	Ledger entries are permanent. Corrections are made through adjustment entries (UC-07). |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream	UC-08, UC-09, UC-10, UC-11, UC-12, UC-13, UC-14, UC-15 Downstream	UC-17 (Evaluate Rank), UC-03 (Notification) |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1	Activity data has been validated by the ingestion use case. AS-2	Scoring rules are configured correctly before processing begins. AS-3	Concurrent requests for the same citizen are handled sequentially to prevent double-counting. |  |  |

UC-17 – Evaluate Citizen Rank

| UC ID and Name: | UC-17 – Evaluate Citizen Rank |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen Trung Anh | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | System (automated) | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | (1) UC-16 awards points to a citizen, OR (2) UC-18 completes year-end processing, OR (3) UC-07 approves a point adjustment, OR (4) Daily scheduled batch evaluation. |  |  |
| Description: | Re-evaluates a citizen's total accumulated points against the configured rank thresholds and assigns the appropriate rank: Active (≥350 points), Basic (100–349 points), or Unranked (\<100 points). If the rank changes from the previous evaluation, the system records the change in rank history, updates the citizen's cached rank, and notifies the citizen whether they were upgraded or downgraded. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1	The citizen has an active account in the system. PRE-2	Rank tier thresholds have been configured by the Administrator (UC-06). PRE-3	The citizen's point balance has been updated by a preceding use case (UC-16, UC-18, or UC-07). |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 The citizen's rank is evaluated and recorded in rank history. POST-2 If the rank changed, the citizen's cached rank is updated and a notification is sent (UC-03). POST-3 If the rank did not change, no further action is taken. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The system receives a rank evaluation request for a citizen.  The system retrieves the citizen's current total points. The system retrieves the active rank tier thresholds configured by the Administrator. The system compares the citizen's total points against the thresholds to determine the appropriate rank: (e.g: Active (≥350), Basic (100–349), or Unranked (\<100)). The system compares the newly determined rank with the citizen's current rank. The rank has changed. The system records a new entry in rank history, including the previous rank, new rank, total points, and timestamp. The system updates the citizen's cached rank for fast retrieval by the mobile app. The system sends a notification to the citizen indicating the new rank and whether it was an upgrade or downgrade (UC-03). |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1	The rank has not changed after evaluation. The system records the evaluation timestamp but takes no further action. No notification is sent. AF-2	Citizen has zero points (new citizen or full reset after year-end). The system assigns Unranked by default. If the citizen was previously ranked, this is recorded as a downgrade. AF-3	Rank is downgraded after year-end carryover (UC-18). The system records the downgrade and notifies the citizen with an explanation that their points were reduced due to the annual 30% retention. AF-4	Rank tier thresholds were recently updated by the Administrator (UC-06). The system uses the latest active thresholds. If the new thresholds cause a rank change, the citizen is notified. AF-5	Batch evaluation: multiple citizens are evaluated in sequence.The system processes each citizen independently. A failure for one citizen does not affect the evaluation of others. AF-6	Citizen is upgraded to Active rank (≥350 points) for the first time.In addition to the standard rank change notification, the citizen is also informed of their new eligibility for the 10% tax/fee reduction (UC-21). |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1	No rank tier thresholds are configured in the system.	            \+The evaluation is skipped. The system logs a critical warning and alerts the Administrator. The citizen's current rank remains unchanged. EX-2	System error during rank history recording.	           \+The evaluation is retried automatically. If retries fail, the Administrator is alerted. The citizen's cached rank may be temporarily outdated until the next successful evaluation. |  |  |
| Priority: | High – Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Very High — triggered every time a citizen's points change (UC-16), after year-end processing (UC-18), after complaint resolution (UC-07), and once daily via scheduled batch. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01	Rank is determined solely by total accumulated points: Active (≥350), Basic (100–349), Unranked (\<100). BR-02	A citizen belongs to exactly one rank at any given time. BR-03	Rank can go up (upgrade) or down (downgrade). Downgrades typically occur after year-end carryover or point adjustments. BR-04	Rank evaluation always uses the latest active tier thresholds. If thresholds are changed by the Administrator, ranks may change at the next evaluation. BR-05	All rank changes are recorded in rank history for audit purposes. BR-06	A citizen with zero points is assigned Unranked by default. BR-07	Only citizens with Active rank (≥350 points) are eligible for the 10% tax/fee reduction incentive. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream	UC-16 (Calculate Points), UC-18 (Year-End Processing), UC-07 (Complaint Resolution) Downstream	UC-03 (Notification), UC-21 (Tax Reduction — when reaching Active rank) |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1	Rank tier thresholds are configured before the system goes live and cover the full point range from zero to the maximum with no gaps. AS-2	The citizen's total points are accurate and consistent with the point ledger at the time of evaluation. AS-3	The notification service is available to deliver rank change alerts promptly. |  |  |

UC-18 – Execute Year-End Processing

| UC ID and Name: | UC-18 – Execute Year-End Processing |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen Trung Anh | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | System (automated — scheduled job) | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | The system scheduler activates on December 31 at the configured time (e.g., 23:59). |  |  |
| Description: | Performs the annual year-end reset for all citizens. The system calculates a carryover based on the configured retention percentage (e.g., 30%) of each citizen's current fiscal year points, rounded down. The annual counter is reset to the carryover amount. The system then re-evaluates each citizen's rank (UC-17) and sends a year-end summary notification (UC-03). This is a once-per-year batch job that affects every citizen in the system. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1	The 30% retention percentage is configured in the system (UC-05). PRE-2	All pending activities for the current fiscal year have been processed by the ingestion and calculation use cases. PRE-3	The system has sufficient capacity to process all citizens within the maintenance window. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 Each citizen's annual point counter is reset to the 30% carryover amount. POST-2 A year-end processing record is created for each citizen, capturing the previous total, carryover amount, and retention percentage applied. POST-3 Each citizen's rank is re-evaluated based on the new carryover balance (UC-17). POST-4 Each citizen receives a year-end summary notification (UC-03). |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The system scheduler triggers the year-end processing job on December 31\. The system retrieves the list of all citizens (active and suspended). For each citizen, the system retrieves their current fiscal year point total. The system calculates the carryover amount: 30% of the current year total, rounded down. The system records the year-end result, including: previous year total, carryover amount, retention percentage, and processing timestamp. The system resets the citizen's annual point counter to the carryover amount. The carryover becomes the starting balance for the new fiscal year. The system triggers rank re-evaluation for the citizen (UC-17). The system sends a year-end summary notification to the citizen (UC-03), showing: previous year total, carryover amount, new rank after reset. After all citizens are processed, the system logs a batch completion summary: total citizens processed, total successful, total failed, processing duration. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1 Citizen has zero points for the current year.	            \+Carryover is zero. Rank remains Unranked (or is downgraded to Unranked if previously ranked). A summary notification is still sent. AF-2 Citizen's account is suspended.	            \+Year-end processing still applies normally. The carryover is calculated, but point accumulation remains paused until the account is restored (UC-20). The citizen still receives the summary notification. AF-3 Carryover causes a rank downgrade (e.g., citizen had Active rank at 400 points, retention is 30%, carryover is 120 points → drops to Basic). 	\+The rank re-evaluation (UC-17) detects the downgrade. The citizen is notified of the downgrade with an explanation that points were reduced due to the annual 30% retention policy. AF-4 Carryover keeps the citizen in the same rank (e.g., citizen had 1,200 points, retention is 30%, carryover is 360 points → remains Active). 	\+Rank does not change. The summary notification shows the new balance without a rank change alert. AF-5 The retention percentage has been changed by the Administrator during the year (e.g., from 30% to 40%).	            \+The system uses the currently configured retention percentage at the time of processing. The actual percentage applied is recorded in the year-end processing record for audit. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1	Processing fails for a specific citizen (e.g., database error). 	\+The system logs the error, skips to the next citizen, and continues processing the batch. After the batch completes, failed citizens are retried automatically. If retries fail, the Administrator is alerted with a list of failed citizens. EX-2	System crash or unexpected shutdown during batch processing.	\+Upon restart, the system identifies which citizens have already been processed (using the year-end processing records) and resumes from where it left off. Citizens already processed are not processed again. EX-3	The retention percentage is not configured in the system.	            \+The processing job is aborted before starting. The system logs a critical error and immediately alerts the Administrator. No citizens are processed until the configuration is corrected. |  |  |
| Priority: | High – Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Once per year — December 31\. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01	The carryover amount is (e.g 30%) of the citizen's current fiscal year total, rounded down to the nearest whole number. For example, a citizen with 451 points carries over 135 points (451 × 0.30 \= 135.3 → 135). BR-02	Year-end processing applies to all citizens regardless of account status (active or suspended). BR-03	The carryover amount becomes the starting balance for the new fiscal year. The citizen's annual counter is reset to this amount, not to zero. BR-04	The retention percentage used is the value configured at the time of processing, not a hardcoded value. BR-05	Each citizen is processed exactly once per year-end cycle. The system must prevent duplicate processing in case of retries or restarts. BR-06	Year-end processing records are retained for audit purposes and cannot be modified after creation. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream	UC-05 (retention percentage configuration), all ingestion and calculation UCs must be complete before year-end runs Downstream	UC-17 (Evaluate Rank), UC-03 (Notification) |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1	All pending activities for the fiscal year have been ingested and calculated before the year-end job triggers. A processing cutoff window is enforced (e.g., no new activities accepted after 23:00 on December 31). AS-2	The system has sufficient capacity to process all citizens (potentially millions) within the overnight maintenance window before the new fiscal year begins. AS-3	The retention percentage is configured and approved by the relevant authority before December 31\. |  |  |

UC-19 – Allocate Vulnerable Group Bonus

| UC ID and Name: | UC-19 – Allocate Vulnerable Group Bonus |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen Trung Anh | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | System (automated) | Secondary Actors: | VNeID Core Identity System |
| Trigger: | (1) Primary: UC-16 finishes calculating points for a vulnerable citizen and the citizen's fiscal year total reaches or exceeds the configured baseline threshold (e.g., 150 points), OR (2) VNeID Core updates a citizen's vulnerable group classification (disability or ethnic minority flag), OR (3) Fallback: Scheduled monthly batch evaluation for all vulnerable citizens to catch any missed triggers. |  |  |
| Description: | Awards an annual bonus (e.g., \+50 points) to citizens who belong to a vulnerable group (elderly over 60, persons with disabilities, ethnic minorities) and have accumulated at least the configured baseline threshold (e.g., 150 points) in the current fiscal year. Vulnerable status is determined by flags from VNeID Core (disability, ethnic minority) or calculated automatically from the citizen's date of birth (elderly over 60). Regardless of how many vulnerable groups a citizen belongs to, the bonus is awarded at most once per fiscal year. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1	The citizen is identified as belonging to at least one vulnerable group, either by a flag from VNeID Core or by the system's own age calculation from the citizen's date of birth. PRE-2	The bonus point value and baseline threshold are configured in the system (UC-05). PRE-3	The citizen has an active account in the system. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 If eligible, the bonus is added to the citizen's point ledger and their balance is updated. POST-2 The citizen is notified of the bonus (UC-03). POST-3 The citizen's rank is re-evaluated based on the new balance (UC-17). POST-4 If not eligible, no action is taken. The citizen may qualify later when conditions are met. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The system determines whether the citizen belongs to a vulnerable group using two methods: (a) checking the disability or ethnic minority flag provided by VNeID Core, OR (b) calculating the citizen's age from their date of birth — if the citizen is over 60 years old, they qualify as elderly. The system retrieves the citizen's current fiscal year point total. The system checks whether the citizen has reached the configured baseline threshold (e.g., 150 points). The system checks whether the bonus has already been awarded to this citizen for the current fiscal year. The citizen is eligible. The system records the bonus in the citizen's point ledger as a separate bonus entry (e.g., \+50 points). The system updates the citizen's point balance. The system triggers rank re-evaluation (UC-17). The system sends a notification to the citizen congratulating them on receiving the vulnerable group bonus (UC-03). |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1	Citizen has not yet reached the baseline threshold.	            \+No bonus is awarded at this time. The system will re-check automatically the next time UC-16 adds points to this citizen. AF-2	Bonus has already been awarded to this citizen for the current fiscal year.	            \+No additional bonus is given, even if the citizen belongs to multiple vulnerable groups (e.g., both elderly and ethnic minority). The system skips this citizen. AF-3	Citizen's account is currently suspended (UC-20).	            \+No bonus is awarded while suspended. If the account is later restored and the citizen meets all other criteria, the bonus can be awarded at that time. AF-4	VNeID Core updates a citizen's vulnerable flag mid-year (e.g., disability certification).	            \+The system recognizes the updated flag. If the citizen already meets the baseline threshold and has not received the bonus, it is awarded immediately. If not yet at threshold, the system monitors via the UC-16 trigger. AF-5	VNeID Core removes a citizen's vulnerable flag (e.g., data correction).	            \+If the bonus has already been awarded for this fiscal year, it is not revoked. If the bonus has not yet been awarded, the citizen is no longer eligible via that flag — but may still qualify through age calculation. AF-6	The bonus would cause the citizen to exceed the overall annual point cap.	            \+The system applies partial points up to the remaining cap, consistent with UC-16 logic. The citizen is notified of the partial award. AF-7	Citizen belongs to multiple vulnerable groups (e.g., over 60 and ethnic minority). Two separate flag update events arrive from VNeID Core.	            \+The system treats all vulnerable group memberships as a single eligibility. The bonus is checked and awarded at most once. The second event triggers the check at Step 4, which detects the bonus has already been awarded, and skips. AF-8	Citizen turns 60 during the fiscal year.           \+The system's age calculation automatically detects this without needing a flag from VNeID Core. If the citizen has already reached the baseline threshold, the bonus is awarded. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1	Vulnerable group flag from VNeID Core is inconsistent or missing for a citizen who should qualify by external records. 	\+The system logs a warning. The Administrator can manually review and correct through UC-07. For elderly citizens, the system can still determine eligibility from date of birth even without a VNeID flag. EX-2	System error during bonus recording.	             \+The operation is cancelled and retried automatically. If retries fail, the Administrator is alerted. The citizen remains eligible for the bonus in subsequent evaluation cycles or the monthly fallback batch. |  |  |
| Priority: | Medium – Should Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | The primary trigger fires in real-time immediately after UC-16 awards points. The monthly batch serves as a fallback to catch any missed cases. Each citizen receives the bonus at most once per fiscal year. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01	Vulnerable groups include: (a) elderly citizens over 60, determined automatically from date of birth, (b) persons with disabilities, determined by VNeID Core flag, and (c) ethnic minorities, determined by VNeID Core flag. BR-02	The baseline threshold to qualify is configurable (e.g., 150 points in the current fiscal year). BR-03	The bonus amount is configurable (e.g., \+50 points). Regardless of how many vulnerable groups a citizen belongs to, the total bonus is awarded at most once per citizen per fiscal year. A citizen who is both elderly and an ethnic minority receives the bonus once, not twice. BR-04	The bonus is recorded as a separate ledger entry with a distinct transaction type (bonus), distinguishable from regular earned points. BR-05	If a citizen's vulnerable flag is removed after the bonus has been awarded, the bonus is not revoked. BR-06	The bonus is subject to the overall annual point cap. If awarding the full bonus would exceed the cap, only the remaining points are awarded. BR-07	Suspended citizens are not eligible for the bonus until their account is restored. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream	UC-16 (primary trigger — after points are calculated), VNeID Core (vulnerable flag updates), UC-05 (bonus amount and threshold configuration) Downstream	UC-17 (Evaluate Rank), UC-03 (Notification) |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1	VNeID Core accurately flags citizens with disabilities and ethnic minority status based on verified government data. AS-2	The citizen's date of birth in the CITIZEN table is accurate and up-to-date, allowing reliable age calculation for elderly determination. AS-3	The bonus amount and baseline threshold are configured by the Administrator before the system begins processing. |  |  |

UC-20 – Suspend/Resume Point Accumulation

| UC ID and Name: | UC-20 – Suspend/Resume Point Accumulation |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen Trung Anh | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | System (automated) | Secondary Actors: | VNeID Core Identity System |
| Trigger: | (1) Suspend: VNeID Core reports that a citizen's account has been locked or revoked due to a violation, OR (2) Resume by VNeID Core: VNeID Core reports that a previously locked account has been restored, OR (3) Auto-resume: The system's daily scheduled job detects that a citizen's time-bound suspension period has expired. |  |  |
| Description: | Manages the pausing and resumption of point accumulation for citizens whose VNeID accounts are locked or revoked. Suspensions are classified into two categories: (a) Time-bound — caused by VCP system rule violations (e.g., spam, abuse), with a configurable duration and auto-resume date; (b) Indefinite — caused by VNeID Core identity-level actions (e.g., account revoked, criminal investigation), with no auto-resume date — only an explicit restore event from VNeID Core can lift an indefinite suspension. When suspended, the citizen's existing points and rank are preserved, but no new points can be earned. Activities occurring during suspension are still logged for audit purposes but earn zero points. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1	The citizen exists in the VCP system. PRE-2	For suspend: VNeID Core has verified and sent a lock/revoke event with a reason code. PRE-3	For resume by VNeID Core: VNeID Core has sent a restore event. PRE-4	For auto-resume: the suspension is classified as time-bound (not indefinite), and the configured duration has expired. PRE-5	Suspension duration policies for each violation level are configured in the system (UC-05). |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 For suspend: the citizen's account status is set to suspended, a suspension record is created with violation details and suspension category (time-bound or indefinite), and the citizen is notified (UC-03). POST-2 For resume: the citizen's account status is set to active, the suspension record is closed with a resume date, and the citizen is notified (UC-03). POST-3 The suspension or resumption event is recorded in the audit log. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The system receives an account status event from VNeID Core, including: citizen VNeID code, event type (lock, revoke, or restore), reason code, and timestamp. The system validates the citizen's identity. The system determines the event type. If the event is a lock or revoke, the system proceeds to Step 4\. If the event is a restore, the system proceeds to Step 8\. The system determines the violation level based on the reason code and classifies the suspension: (a) VCP rule violation → time-bound, with a configurable duration and auto-resume date. (b) Identity-level lock → indefinite, with no auto-resume date. The system creates a suspension record with: violation details, suspension category, duration and auto-resume date (if time-bound). The system updates the citizen's account status to suspended. The system notifies the citizen: for time-bound, the notification includes the expected resume date; for indefinite, the notification states that resumption requires VNeID account restoration (UC-03). The system logs the event. Flow ends. The system confirms the citizen is currently suspended. The system closes the active suspension record by recording the resume date and source. The system updates the citizen's account status to active. 11	The system notifies the citizen that their point accumulation has resumed (UC-03). The system logs the event. Flow ends. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1	Citizen is already suspended and a new lock/revoke event arrives.	           The system creates a new suspension record. If the new violation has a longer remaining duration, the auto-resume date is extended. If the new violation is identity-level, the suspension is reclassified to indefinite and the auto-resume date is removed. The citizen is notified of the update. AF-2	Restore event arrives but the citizen is not currently suspended.	The system ignores the event and logs a warning. No action is taken. AF-3	Activities are recorded for a suspended citizen by ingestion UCs (UC-08 through UC-15). 	Activities are stored with a rejection reason. No points are calculated. Records are preserved for audit. AF-4	A time-bound suspension expires (auto-resume date reached). The daily scheduled job detects this. 	The system automatically resumes the citizen by executing Steps 8–11. Indefinite suspensions are skipped entirely by the scheduled job. AF-5	VNeID Core sends a restore event before the auto-resume date (early restoration). 	The system resumes immediately via Steps 8–11. The auto-resume date becomes irrelevant. AF-6	Citizen has pending vulnerable group bonus eligibility (UC-19) at suspension time. 	The bonus is not awarded while suspended. If later resumed and still eligible, the bonus can be awarded at that time. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1	Network failure between VNeID Core and VCP. 	VNeID Core retries. If retries fail, the event is queued. The citizen's status may be temporarily inconsistent. EX-2	Unknown reason code received from VNeID Core. 	The system defaults to indefinite suspension for safety. The Administrator is alerted to review. EX-3	System error during auto-resume batch. 	The system skips to the next citizen and continues. Failed citizens are retried in the next daily batch. |  |  |
| Priority: | High – Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Low — suspensions occur only when violations happen. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01	Suspended citizens cannot earn any new points. Activities during suspension are logged but not rewarded. BR-02	Existing points and rank are preserved during suspension. Only new accumulation is paused. BR-03	Suspensions are classified into: (a) Time-bound — VCP rule violations, configurable duration, auto-resume date. (b) Indefinite — VNeID identity-level locks, no auto-resume date, requires explicit VNeID Core restore. BR-04	Time-bound duration is configurable by violation level (e.g., minor \= 30 days, moderate \= 90 days, severe \= 365 days). BR-05	New violations during suspension extend the duration or reclassify to indefinite if identity-level. BR-06	The auto-resume job only processes time-bound suspensions. Indefinite suspensions are never auto-resumed. BR-07	Suspension and resumption events are recorded for audit and cannot be modified. BR-08	Year-end processing (UC-18) still applies to suspended citizens. Carryover is calculated, but no new points until resumed. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream	VNeID Core (lock/revoke/restore events), UC-05 (suspension duration configuration) Downstream	UC-03 (Notification), UC-16 (blocks point calculation), UC-19 (blocks bonus) |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1	VNeID Core provides timely and accurate notifications with valid reason codes. AS-2	Suspension duration policies are configured before the system goes live. AS-3	The daily auto-resume job runs reliably. AS-4	VNeID Core's reason codes clearly distinguish VCP-level violations from identity-level locks. |  |  |

UC-21 – Determine Tax/Fee Reduction Tier

| UC ID and Name: | UC-21 – Determine Tax/Fee Reduction Tier |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen Trung Anh | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | Citizen | Secondary Actors: | None |
| Trigger: | A Citizen navigates to the incentive eligibility section in the VNeID mobile app. |  |  |
| Description: | Displays the citizen's specific tax and fee reduction eligibility based on their current rank. Citizens who have achieved the configured highest rank (e.g., Active ≥350 points) are eligible for a configured reduction percentage (e.g., 10%) on Personal Income Tax (PIT), Registration Fees, and/or Value-Added Tax (VAT). Citizens at lower ranks are shown their current standing and how many more points they need to reach the eligible rank. The reduction percentages and rank thresholds are configurable by the Administrator (UC-06) and may vary by tax type. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1	The Citizen is authenticated (UC-01). PRE-2	The Citizen's rank has been evaluated (UC-17). PRE-3	Incentive rates and rank thresholds have been configured by the Administrator (UC-06). |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1 The citizen's incentive eligibility is displayed, showing either the applicable reductions or guidance on how to qualify. |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The Citizen navigates to the incentive eligibility section in the VNeID app.  The system retrieves the citizen's current rank and total points.  The system retrieves the configured incentive rates and rank thresholds (UC-06).  The system maps the citizen's rank to the applicable reduction rates. The citizen holds the configured highest rank eligible for reductions. The system displays the eligible reductions, showing for each tax type (PIT, Registration Fee, VAT): the reduction percentage, a brief description, and the eligibility status.   The system also displays the citizen's current total points and a note that maintaining the eligible rank is required to continue receiving the reductions. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1	Citizen's rank is below the configured eligible rank. 	The system displays that the citizen does not currently qualify for tax/fee reductions. The system shows the citizen's current points, the points needed to reach the eligible rank (configured threshold − current points), and a list of suggested activities that could help them earn more points. AF-2	Citizen's rank is the configured lowest rank. 	Same as AF-1, but the system also shows intermediate rank milestones as stepping stones toward the eligible rank. AF-3	Citizen's account is suspended (UC-20). 	The system displays the citizen's last known rank and points, with a notice that incentive eligibility is paused during suspension. No reduction percentages are shown as eligible. AF-4	Incentive rates have not yet been configured by the Administrator. 	The system displays a message that incentive information is not yet available. No reduction details are shown. AF-5	Citizen holds the eligible rank and the incentive data has already been exported to the Tax Authority for the current period (UC-22). 	The system displays the eligible reductions along with the export status (e.g., "Your reduction eligibility has been submitted to the Tax Authority on \[date\]. Awaiting confirmation."). AF-6	Citizen holds the eligible rank and the Tax Authority has confirmed the reduction (UC-22 import). 	The system displays the confirmed reductions with the actual reduction amounts applied, effective date, and confirmation source. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1	System cannot retrieve the citizen's rank or incentive data. 	An error message is displayed, asking the citizen to try again later. EX-2	Incentive configuration data is inconsistent (e.g., missing reduction for a specific tax type). 	The system displays available reductions and shows "information unavailable" for the missing tax type. The system logs a warning for the Administrator.  |  |  |
| Priority: | Priority: Medium – Should Have. |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Medium — citizens check their incentive eligibility periodically, especially near tax filing periods. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01	Only citizens who hold the configured highest rank (e.g., Active ≥350 points) are eligible for tax/fee reductions. The rank name and threshold are defined by the Administrator (UC-06). BR-02	Reduction percentages are configurable by the Administrator (UC-06) and may vary by tax type (e.g., PIT 10%, Registration Fee 10%, VAT 10%). BR-03	Incentive eligibility is determined by the citizen's current rank at the time of viewing. If the rank changes, the eligibility changes accordingly. BR-04	Displaying eligibility does not guarantee the reduction has been applied. The actual application depends on the export/import process with the Tax Authority (UC-22). BR-05	Suspended citizens are not shown as eligible for reductions, even if their point total would otherwise qualify. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream	UC-01 (Authentication), UC-17 (Evaluate Rank), UC-06 (Incentive Configuration), UC-22 (Export/Import status) Downstream	UC-22 (citizen eligibility feeds into the export to Tax Authority) |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1	Incentive rates and rank thresholds have been configured by the Administrator before citizens access this feature. AS-2	The citizen's rank is up-to-date at the time of viewing. AS-3	The VNeID app displays the incentive information in a clear and accessible format compliant with accessibility standards (NF-08). |  |  |

UC-22 – Export/Import Incentive Data

| UC ID and Name: | UC-22 – Export/Import Incentive Data |  |  |
| ----: | :---- | ----: | :---- |
| Created By: | Nguyen Trung Anh | Date Created: | 05/03/2026 |
| Primary Actor: | Tax and Treasury Authority | Secondary Actors: | System (automated — scheduled job) |
| Trigger: | (1) Configured scheduled cycle (e.g., monthly or quarterly) or manual trigger by Administrator for export, OR (2) Tax Authority sends confirmation data back to the VCP system after applying the reductions. |  |  |
| Description: | Handles the bi-directional data exchange between the VCP system and the Tax and Treasury Authority. The export compiles a list of eligible citizens (holding the configured highest rank, not suspended, and having granted data-sharing consent), transmits their reduction details to the Tax Authority. The import receives confirmation from the Tax Authority after reductions have been applied, updates the incentive history, and notifies each confirmed citizen. Eligibility is determined at the time of export — subsequent rank changes or suspensions do not affect pending confirmations. This use case complies with Decree 13/2023/NĐ-CP on personal data protection by requiring explicit citizen consent before sharing data with external authorities. |  |  |
| Preconditions: | PRE-1	Citizens have been evaluated and assigned ranks (UC-17). PRE-2	Incentive rates and rank thresholds are configured (UC-06). PRE-3	The secure API connection between VCP and Tax Authority is available. PRE-4	For import: an export has been previously sent for the corresponding period. |  |  |
| Postconditions: | POST-1	For export: the eligible citizen list is transmitted to the Tax Authority and an export record is logged for audit. POST-2	For import: each citizen's incentive history is updated with the confirmation and the citizen is notified (UC-03). |  |  |
| Normal Flow: | The system receives a data exchange request. This is either (a) an export trigger (scheduled cycle or manual by Administrator), or (b) an import trigger (confirmation data received from Tax Authority). If export, proceed to Step 2\. If import, proceed to Step 9\. The system retrieves the configured eligible rank and corresponding reduction percentages (UC-06). The system compiles a list of all citizens who currently hold the eligible rank, whose accounts are not suspended, and who have explicitly granted consent to share their data with the Tax Authority (compliant with Decree 13/2023/NĐ-CP). For each eligible citizen, the system prepares an export record containing: citizen identifier, current rank, current total points, and the applicable reduction percentage for each tax type (PIT, Registration Fee, VAT). The system creates an export batch record with: fiscal year, export date, total number of records, and status \= pending. The system transmits the export data to the Tax Authority via secure API. Upon successful transmission, the system updates the export batch status to sent. The system logs the export event in the audit log. Flow ends. The system receives confirmation data from the Tax Authority, containing: citizen identifier, tax type, reduction amount applied, effective date, and confirmation reference. 1For each confirmation record, the system matches the citizen against the original export batch to verify the citizen was included in a prior export. 1The system records the confirmation details in the citizen's incentive history. 1The system sends a notification to the citizen confirming that their tax/fee reduction has been applied (UC-03). The system updates the export batch status to confirmed or partially confirmed. The system logs the import event in the audit log. Flow ends. |  |  |
| Alternative Flows: | AF-1	No citizens are eligible for export (none hold the eligible rank, or none have granted consent). 	The system generates an empty export with zero records, logs it for audit, and notifies the Administrator. No data is transmitted. AF-2	A citizen's rank changed between the export date and the confirmation date (e.g., downgraded after year-end).	             The confirmation is still accepted for the period it covers, since the citizen was eligible at the time of export. A note is recorded indicating the citizen's rank has since changed. AF-3	Tax Authority sends confirmation for a citizen not included in any prior export. 	The system rejects the confirmation record and logs a warning. The Administrator is alerted to investigate. AF-4	Tax Authority sends a duplicate confirmation for the same citizen, tax type, and period. 	The system detects the duplicate and skips it. A warning is logged. AF-5	A citizen is suspended between the export date and the confirmation date. 	The confirmation is still accepted, since the citizen was eligible at the time of export. Suspension affects future eligibility, not retroactive confirmations. AF-6	Administrator manually triggers a re-export for the same period.	The system generates a supplementary export containing only citizens who were not included in the previous export for the same period and who have granted consent. The previous export remains valid. AF-7	A citizen who holds the eligible rank has not granted data-sharing consent. 	The citizen is excluded from the export. The system may optionally notify the citizen via UC-03 that they are eligible for tax reductions but need to grant consent to receive them. |  |  |
| Exceptions: | EX-1	Network failure during export transmission. 	The export is retried automatically. If all retries fail, the export status is set to failed and the Administrator is alerted. The export can be retried manually. EX-2	Tax Authority sends confirmation data with invalid format. 	Invalid records are rejected. Valid records continue processing. The Administrator is notified with a list of rejected records. EX-3	System error during import processing (partial failure). 	Successfully processed records are committed. Failed records are retried in the next import cycle. The Administrator is alerted. |  |  |
| Priority: | Priority: High – Must Have |  |  |
| Frequency of Use: | Low — export runs on a configured schedule (e.g., monthly or quarterly). Import occurs when the Tax Authority responds. |  |  |
| Business Rules: | BR-01	Only citizens who hold the configured eligible rank, are not suspended, and have granted explicit data-sharing consent are included in the export. BR-02	Each export is logged with timestamp, total record count, and fiscal year for audit. Export records cannot be modified after creation. BR-03	Confirmation imports are matched against original export records. Confirmations for citizens not in any prior export are rejected. BR-04	A citizen can receive confirmations for multiple tax types independently. Each tax type confirmation is recorded separately. BR-05	Eligibility is determined at the time of export, not at the time of confirmation. Subsequent rank changes or suspensions do not affect pending confirmations. BR-06	Duplicate confirmations for the same citizen, tax type, and period are detected and skipped. BR-07	Supplementary exports include only citizens not previously exported for the same period and who have granted consent. BR-08	Data sharing with the Tax Authority complies with Decree 13/2023/NĐ-CP on personal data protection. Citizen consent is required before any data is transmitted to external authorities. |  |  |
| Other Information: | Upstream	UC-17 (Evaluate Rank), UC-06 (Incentive Configuration) Downstream	UC-03 (Notification) |  |  |
| Assumptions: | AS-1	The Tax Authority provides timely confirmation after applying reductions. AS-2	The secure API between VCP and Tax Authority supports encrypted data exchange compliant with NF-05. AS-3	The data format is agreed upon and documented before the system goes live. AS-4	The export schedule is configured and aligned with the Tax Authority's processing cycles. AS-5	Citizens are provided a clear mechanism to grant or revoke data-sharing consent within the VNeID app. |  |  |

**Functional Description Contents**  
***Use Case ID and Name***  
Give each use case a unique integer sequence number identifier. State a concise name for the use case that indicates the value the use case would provide to some user. Begin with an action verb, followed by an object.  
***Author and Date Created***  
Enter the name of the person who initially wrote this use case and the date it was written.  
***Primary and Secondary Actors***  
An actor is a person or other entity external to the software system being specified who interacts with the system and performs use cases to accomplish tasks. Different actors often correspond to different user classes, or roles, identified from the customer community that will use the product. Name the primary actor that will be initiating this use case and any other secondary actors who will participate in completing execution of the use case.  
***Trigger***  
Identify the business event, system event, or user action that initiates the use case. This trigger alerts the system that it should begin testing the preconditions for the use case so it can judge whether to proceed with execution.  
***Description***  
Provide a brief description of the reason for and outcome of this use case, or a high-level description of the sequence of actions and the outcome of executing the use case.  
***Preconditions***  
List any activities that must take place, or any conditions that must be true, before the use case can be started. The system must be able to test each precondition. Number each precondition. Example: PRE-1: User’s identity has been authenticated.  
***Postconditions***  
Describe the state of the system at the successful conclusion of the use case execution. Label each postcondition in the form POST-X, where X is a sequence number. Example: POST-1: Price of item in the database has been updated with the new value.  
***Normal Flow***  
Provide a description of the user actions and corresponding system responses that will take place during execution of the use case under normal, expected conditions. This dialog sequence will ultimately lead to accomplishing the goal stated in the use case name and description. Show a numbered list of actions performed by the actor, alternating with responses provided by the system. The normal flow is numbered “X.0”, where “X” is the Use Case ID.  
***Alternative Flows***  
Document other successful usage scenarios that can take place within this use case. State the alternative flow, and describe any differences in the sequence of steps that take place. Number each alternative flow in the form “X.Y”, where “X” is the Use Case ID and Y is a sequence number for the alternative flow. For example, “5.3” would indicate the third alternative flow for use case number 5\. Indicate where each alternative flow would branch off from the normal flow, and if pertinent, where it would rejoin the normal flow.  
***Exceptions***  
Describe any anticipated error conditions that could occur during execution of the use case and how the system is to respond to those conditions. Number each alternative flow in the form “X.Y.EZ”, where “X” is the Use Case ID, Y indicates the normal (0) or alternative (\>0) flow during which this exception could take place, “E” indicates an exception, and “Z” is a sequence number for the exceptions. For example “5.0.E2” would indicate the second exception for the normal flow for use case number 5\. Indicate where in the normal (or an alternative) flow each exception could occur.  
***Priority***  
Indicate the relative priority of implementing the functionality required to allow this use case to be executed. Use the same priority scheme as that used for the functional requirements.  
***Frequency of Use***  
Estimate the number of times this use case will be performed per some appropriate unit of time. This gives an early indicator of throughput, concurrent usage loads, and transaction capacity.  
***Business Rules***  
List any business rules that influence this use case. Don’t include the business rule text here, just its identifier so the reader can find it in another repository when needed.  
***Other Information***  
Identify any additional requirements, such as quality attributes, for the use case that may need to be addressed during design or implementation. Also list any associated functional requirements that aren’t a direct part of the use case flows but which a developer needs to know about. Describe what should happen if the use case execution fails for some unanticipated or systemic reason (e.g., loss of network connectivity, timeout). If the use case results in a durable state change in a database or the outside world, state whether the change is rolled back, completed correctly, partially completed with a known state, or left in an undetermined state as a result of the exception.  
***Assumptions***  
List any assumptions that were made regarding this use case or how it might execute.

#### ***b. Business Rules***

Provide the business rules those are applied only to the use case

| ID | Business Rule | Business Rule Description |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| UC01-BR01 | Unique Digital Identity | Each citizen has only one unique digital identity linked to their national electronic identification account, protected by law. |
| UC01-BR02 | Security Responsibility | Citizens are responsible for securing their electronic identification account information and authentication methods. |
| UC01-BR03 | Legal Validity | Electronic identification and authentication using VNeID have legal validity for executing digital transactions. |
| UC01-BR04 | Data Consent Compliance | Citizen consent to share identity data is required at first login and is compliant with Decree 13/2023/NĐ-CP. |
| UC01-BR05 | Session Timeout Policy | Admin session timeout must be configured shorter than Citizen session timeout (e.g., Admin 15 min, Citizen 30 min). |
| UC01-BR06 | Failed Attempt Logging | All failed login attempts are recorded in the AUDIT\_LOG with timestamp, IP address, and failure reason. |
| UC01-BR07 | Suspension Read-Only Access | Suspended citizens can authenticate and access read-only features but cannot perform point-earning activities. |
| UC01-BR08 | Admin Account Lockdown | Admin accounts are temporarily locked after exceeding the configured maximum failed login attempts. |
| UC01-BR09 | Unique Matching Key | The Citizen Identity Number (Số CCCD) is the sole unique identifier used to match and deduplicate records. |
| UC02-BR01 | Highest Rank Threshold | The highest rank (Active Digital Citizen) is determined by the maximum tier threshold (e.g., $\\ge 350$ points). |
| UC02-BR02 | Intermediate Rank Threshold | The intermediate rank (Basic Digital Citizen) is determined by the mid-tier threshold (e.g., $100$ – $349$ points). |
| UC02-BR03 | Minimum Rank Threshold | The lowest status (Unranked) is determined by the minimum tier threshold (e.g., $\< 100$ points). |
| UC03-BR01 | Reason Transparency | Notifications must clearly and transparently state the exact reason for any point changes. |
| UC03-BR02 | Notification Privacy | Notifications must comply with data protection and not expose sensitive info on the lock screen. |
| UC03-BR03 | Notification Aggregation | Multiple events within a 5-minute window may be aggregated into a single summary message to avoid spam. |
| UC04-BR01 | Attachment Standards | Supporting files must not exceed 10MB and must be in PDF, JPG, PNG, or DOCX formats. |
| UC04-BR02 | Pending Submission Limit | Each Citizen is allowed a maximum of 3 complaints/feedback submissions in "Pending" status at any time. |
| UC04-BR03 | Incentivized Feedback | Points are awarded (5 points) only when a submission is marked as "Successfully Processed" and flagged as "Constructive". |
| UC05-BR01 | Positive Integer Values | Configured point values must be positive integers; the system does not apply negative point rules. |
| UC05-BR02 | Soft Deletion Only | Hard deleting a rule already used to generate points is prohibited; it can only be deactivated (Soft Delete). |
| UC05-BR03 | Max Retention Configuration | Rules eligible for retention can only be configured up to the maximum allowed rate (e.g., 30%). |
| UC05-BR04 | Rule Versioning Control | Modifying an active rule creates a new version with "Valid From/To" dates to preserve audit history. |
| UC05-BR05 | Maker-Checker Protocol | Any addition or modification to scoring rules requires dual authorization (Maker and Checker). |
| UC06-BR01 | Threshold Exclusivity | Rank thresholds must be continuous and mutually exclusive to ensure a citizen belongs to only one rank. |
| UC06-BR02 | Non-retroactivity Policy | Changes to incentive configurations apply strictly to future transactions and never alter past history. |
| UC06-BR03 | Tier-Bound Incentives | Incentive policies are strictly bound to the current rank; higher tiers receive maximum configured benefits. |
| UC07a-BR01 | Mandatory Resolution Feedback | Any resolution decision must include a "Reason/Feedback" entry for transparency and audit trail. |
| UC07b-BR01 | Report Data Anonymization | Data within the reporting module must have all sensitive PII anonymized, displaying only aggregated data. |
| UC08-BR01 | One-time Lifecycle Reward | Profile completion activity is recorded and eligible for reward only once per citizen's lifetime. |
| UC09-BR01 | Unique Document Reward | A citizen can only earn points once per specific document type integrated into VNeID. |
| UC10-BR01 | Engagement Rate-Limiting | Rate-limiting is applied to engagement logs to prevent point farming via automated scripts. |
| UC11-BR02 | Transaction Idempotency | Each unique transaction reference from public services is recorded once to guarantee idempotency. |
| UC12-BR01 | Authentication Trace Rule | Financial transactions must carry a valid VNeID authentication trace or they are rejected. |
| UC13-BR01 | PHI Privacy Restriction | System must only process metadata; it must NEVER store actual medical diagnoses or sensitive Health Information. |
| UC14-BR01 | Approved Course Mapping | Only courses explicitly approved and mapped in the system configurations are eligible for literacy points. |
| UC15-BR01 | Civic Participation Uniqueness | Civic participation is counted once per specific draft, survey, or interaction (via document ID). |
| UC16-BR01 | Single Count Rule | Each activity is counted only once per citizen per date. |
| UC16-BR02 | Annual Frequency Limit | Each activity type has a maximum frequency per fiscal year. |
| UC16-BR05 | Partial Point Awarding | When a cap is partially reached, remaining points are awarded rather than rejecting the entire activity. |
| UC16-BR08 | Permanent Ledger Entries | Ledger entries are permanent; corrections must be made through adjustment entries (UC-07). |
| UC17-BR01 | Points-Based Ranking | Rank is determined solely by total points: Active ($\\ge 350$), Basic ($100$–$349$), Unranked ($\< 100$). |
| UC17-BR07 | Incentive Eligibility Rank | Only citizens with Active rank ($\\ge 350$ points) are eligible for the 10% tax/fee reduction. |
| UC18-BR01 | Annual Carryover Formula | Carryover is 30% of current fiscal year total points, rounded down to the nearest whole number. |
| UC18-BR03 | Starting Balance Reset | The carryover amount becomes the starting balance for the new fiscal year, not zero. |
| UC18-BR05 | Deduplication Prevention | Each citizen is processed exactly once per year-end cycle to prevent duplicate processing. |
| UC19-BR01 | Vulnerable Group Definition | Includes elderly (\>60), persons with disabilities, and ethnic minorities (verified by VNeID). |
| UC19-BR03 | Single Annual Bonus | The vulnerable bonus is awarded at most once per citizen per fiscal year regardless of group count. |
| UC19-BR05 | Flag Persistence Rule | If a vulnerable flag is removed after the bonus is awarded, the bonus is not revoked. |
| UC20-BR01 | Accumulation Pause | Suspended citizens preserve existing points/rank but cannot earn new points. |
| UC20-BR03 | Suspension Classification | Categorized as Time-bound (configurable duration) or Indefinite (requires VNeID restore). |
| UC21-BR04 | Eligibility vs. Application | Displaying eligibility does not guarantee reduction; actual application depends on Tax Authority export/import. |
| UC22-BR01 | Export Eligibility Criteria | Includes only citizens with eligible rank, active status, and explicit data-sharing consent. |
| UC22-BR05 | Export Time Snapshot | Eligibility is determined at export; subsequent rank changes or suspensions do not affect pending confirmations. |

# **IV. Design specification** {#iv.-design-specification}

## IV.1 Integrated Communication Diagrams  {#iv.1-integrated-communication-diagrams}

*Sample*  
*![][image85]*

1. ***Diagram 1: Tương tác của Công dân (Citizen Interactions)***   
   * ***Bao gồm các UC:** UC-01 (Authenticate User), UC-02 (Inquire Point Balance and Rank), UC-03 (Receive System Notifications), UC-04 (Submit Feedback and Complaint), UC-21 (Determine Tax/Fee Reduction Tier).*   
   * ***Mục tiêu:** Mô tả luồng tương tác trực tiếp của người dùng Citizen với hệ thống VCP qua ứng dụng VNeID.*

*![][image86]*

2. ***Diagram 2: Tiếp nhận Dữ liệu và Ghi nhận Hoạt động (Data Ingestion and Recording)***  
   * ***Bao gồm các UC:** UC-08 (Record Profile Completion), UC-09 (Record Document Integration), UC-10 (Record App Engagement), UC-11 (Record Public Service Completion), UC-12 (Record Financial Transactions), UC-13 (Record Healthcare and Donation), UC-14 (Record Digital Literacy Completion), UC-15 (Record Civic Engagement). \- Sơn \+ Quy*  
   * ***Mục tiêu:** Mô tả luồng dữ liệu vào hệ thống VCP từ tất cả các Hệ thống Ngoài (External Systems) như VNeID Core, DVC Portal, Financial Partner, Health/Edu/Legislative Platform.*

*![][image87]*

3. ***Diagram 3: Xử lý Logic và Phân hạng Nội bộ (Internal System Core Logic and Ranking)***  
   * ***Bao gồm các UC:** UC-16 (Calculate and Add Points), UC-17 (Evaluate Citizen Rank), UC-19 (Allocate Vulnerable Group Bonus), UC-20 (Suspend/Resume Accumulation), UC-18 (Execute Year-End Processing). \-Hiếu*

   ***Mục tiêu:** Mô tả các quy trình tính toán, phân hạng, xử lý thưởng/phạt và xử lý cuối năm diễn ra hoàn toàn bên trong hệ thống VCP.*

     *![][image88]*

4. ***Diagram 4: Quản trị và Trao đổi Dữ liệu Thuế (Administration and Tax Authority Exchange)***  
   * ***Bao gồm các UC:** UC-05 (Configure Scoring Rules), UC-06 (Configure Tiered Incentives), UC-07 (Resolve Complaints and View Reports), UC-22 (Export/Import Incentive Data). \- TA*  
   * ***Mục tiêu:** Mô tả các luồng chức năng quản lý bởi System Administrator và luồng trao đổi dữ liệu ưu đãi (Tax/Fee Incentives) với Hệ thống Ngoài (Tax & Treasury Authority).*

*![][image89]*

# **V. Implementation**  {#v.-implementation}

## V.1 Map architecture to the structure of the project  {#v.1-map-architecture-to-the-structure-of-the-project}

*\[Provide:* 

1. ***Overview of the Chosen Architecture***

*Briefly describe the architectural style (e.g., Layered, MVC, Service-Oriented Architecture)*  
*Explain **why it was selected** for this project (e.g., to meet non-functional requirements like maintainability, scalability, reusability)*

2. ***Mapping to Project Structure***

*Present the **actual project folder/package structure***  
*Map each layer/component in the architecture to concrete project modules\]*

## V.2 Map Class Diagram and Interaction Diagram to Code {#v.2-map-class-diagram-and-interaction-diagram-to-code}

*\[Demo the implementation of several features using design patterns\]*

# **VI. Applying Alternative Architecture Patterns** {#vi.-applying-alternative-architecture-patterns}

## VI.1 Applying the ………….. (ex: ***Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA))***  architecture {#vi.1-applying-the-…………..-(ex:-service-oriented-architecture-(soa))-architecture}

*\[Redesign part of the system architecture using **Service-Oriented Architecture (SOA)** to improve **reusability**, **scalability**, and **modularity**.*  
***Students must provide:***

1. ***Problem Identification***

*Briefly describe which non-functional requirement(s) are **not fully supported** by the current architecture (e.g., NF-05: Reusability)*

2. ***SOA-Based Solution***

*Reorganize system components into independent services*  
*E.g., refactor FSOData into separate service modules*

3. ***Supporting Diagrams***  
   *Deployment Diagram*  
   *Updated Class Diagram*  
   *Component Diagram showing separate services\]*  
   

## VI.2 Applying Service Discovery Pattern in the service-oriented architecture  {#vi.2-applying-service-discovery-pattern-in-the-service-oriented-architecture}

*\[Support dynamic service registration and discovery in **SOA or microservice-based architecture** for **horizontal scalability** and ease of expansion.*  
 ***Students must provide:***

1. ***Problem & Requirement***

*Explain the relevant non-functional requirement (e.g., NF-06: Scalability)*  
*Describe the need for service discovery in a growing system (e.g., multiple branches, independent modules)*

2. ***Service Discovery-Based Solution***

*Integrate a **Service Discovery Pattern***  
*Mention possible technologies: **Consul**, **Eureka**, **Kubernetes DNS***

3. ***Supporting Diagrams***

*Deployment Diagram (with multiple instances and registry)*  
*Updated Class/Component Diagram including a Discovery Service\]*
